133\u2192    // Wait for AI events\n   134\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   135\u2192      () => {\n   136\u2192        const log = document.getElementById('event-log');\n   137\u2192        return log && (log.textContent.match(/AI/g) || []).length >= 1;\n   138\u2192      },\n   139\u2192      { timeout: 15_000 }\n   140\u2192    );\n   141\u2192    const logText = await page.locator('#event-log').innerText();\n   142\u2192    // At least one entry should mention a character name\n   143\u2192    const hasCharName = /(Maple|Sol|Nyx)/.test(logText);\n   144\u2192    expect(hasCharName, 'Event log should mention a character name').toBe(true);\n   145\u2192  });\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192  test('event log entries include object state transitions (auto events)', async ({ page }) => {\n   148\u2192    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n   149\u2192    // Wait for an auto object state transition to appear in the log\n   150\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   151\u2192      () => {\n   152\u2192        const log = document.getElementById('event-log');\n   153\u2192        return log && log.textContent.includes('auto');\n   154\u2192      },\n   155\u2192      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }\n   156\u2192    );\n   157\u2192    const logText = await page.locator('#event-log').innerText();\n   158\u2192    expect(logText).toMatch(/auto/);\n   159\u2192    // Should show idle \u2192 something transition\n   160\u2192    expect(logText).toMatch(/idle|brewing|cooking|tending|reading|watching|sleeping|working|painting/i);\n   161\u2192  });\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192  test('object badge updates from idle to active when triggered', async ({ page }) => {\n   164\u2192    // Trigger bonsai via use button\n   165\u2192    const useBtn = page.locator('#int-bonsai button');\n   166\u2192    const bonsaiBadge = page.locator('#badge-bonsai');\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192    // Confirm starts idle\n   169\u2192    await expect(bonsaiBadge).toHaveText('idle');\n   170\u2192    await useBtn.click();\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    // Badge should change from idle\n   173\u2192    await expect(bonsaiBadge).not.toHaveText('idle', { timeout: 5000 });\n   174\u2192    const newState = await bonsaiBadge.innerText();\n   175\u2192    expect(newState).not.toBe('idle');\n   176\u2192    console.log(`Bonsai transitioned to: ${newState}`);\n   177\u2192  });\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192  test('active object shows timer countdown text (\"Xs remaining\")', async ({ page }) => {\n   180\u2192    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n   181\u2192    // Wait for any object to show a timer\n   182\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   183\u2192      () => {\n   184\u2192        const timerTexts = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"timertext-\"]');\n   185\u2192        return Array.from(timerTexts).some(el => el.textContent.includes('remaining'));\n   186\u2192      },\n   187\u2192      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }\n   188\u2192    );\n   189\u2192    const timerTexts = await page.evaluate(() =>\n   190\u2192      Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"timertext-\"]'))\n   191\u2192        .filter(el => el.textContent.includes('remaining'))\n   192\u2192        .map(el => ({ id: el.id, text: el.textContent }))\n   193\u2192    );\n   194\u2192    expect(timerTexts.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   195\u2192    console.log('Active object timers:', timerTexts);\n   196\u2192  });\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192  test('character card shows \"Hold Xs\" when character is interacting', async ({ page }) => {\n   199\u2192    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n   200\u2192    // Wait for a character to show Hold text (means they are actively interacting)\n   201\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   202\u2192      () => {\n   203\u2192        const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n   204\u2192        return Array.from(cards).some(c => /Hold\\s+\\d+s/.test(c.textContent));\n   205\u2192      },\n   206\u2192      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }\n   207\u2192    );\n   208\u2192    const holdCards = await page.evaluate(() =>\n   209\u2192      Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]'))\n   210\u2192        .filter(c => /Hold\\s+\\d+s/.test(c.textContent))\n   211\u2192        .map(c => {\n   212\u2192          const nameMatch = c.textContent.match(/(Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);\n   213\u2192          const holdMatch = c.textContent.match(/Hold\\s+(\\d+)s/);\n   214\u2192          return {\n   215\u2192            name: nameMatch?.[1],\n   216\u2192            hold: holdMatch?.[1] ? parseInt(holdMatch[1]) : null,\n   217\u2192          };\n   218\u2192        })\n   219\u2192    );\n   220\u2192    expect(holdCards.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   221\u2192    for (const card of holdCards) {\n   222\u2192      expect(card.hold, `${card.name}: Hold duration should be > 0`).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   223\u2192    }\n   224\u2192    console.log('Interacting characters:', holdCards);\n   225\u2192  });\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192  test('character card shows \"AI next Xs\" countdown for upcoming decision', async ({ page }) => {\n   228\u2192    const aiNextText = await page.waitForFunction(\n   229\u2192      () => {\n   230\u2192        const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n   231\u2192        return Array.from(cards).some(c => /AI next\\s+\\d+s/.test(c.textContent));\n   232\u2192      },\n   233\u2192      { timeout: 15_000 }\n   234\u2192    );\n   235\u2192    const cards = await page.evaluate(() =>\n   236\u2192      Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]'))\n   237\u2192        .map(c => ({\n   238\u2192          id: c.id,\n   239\u2192          aiNext: c.textContent.match(/AI next\\s+(\\d+)s/)?.[1] ?? null,\n   240\u2192        }))\n   241\u2192    );\n   242\u2192    const withAiNext = cards.filter(c => c.aiNext !== null);\n   243\u2192    expect(withAiNext.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   244\u2192    for (const c of withAiNext) {\n   245\u2192      const seconds = parseInt(c.aiNext);\n   246\u2192      expect(seconds, `${c.id}: AI next should be \u2264 30s`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(30);\n   247\u2192    }\n   248\u2192    console.log('AI next countdown:', withAiNext);\n   249\u2192  });\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 PAUSE / RESUME \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192  test('Pause button stops the sim clock', async ({ page }) => {\n   254\u2192    const time1 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n   255\u2192    await page.locator('#btn-pause').click();\n   256\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(3000);\n   257\u2192    const time2 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n   258\u2192    expect(time2, 'Clock should be frozen when paused').toBe(time1);\n   259\u2192    // Resume\n   260\u2192    await page.locator('#btn-pause').click();\n   261\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n   262\u2192    const time3 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n   263\u2192    expect(time3, 'Clock should advance after resume').not.toBe(time2);\n   264\u2192  });\n   265\u2192});\n SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-209
     4→ * M1 Test Plan: Activity Duration & 30s AI Cycle
     5→ *
     6→ * Verifies:
     7→ *  1. Characters stay at objects for the correct activity duration
     8→ *  2. interactTimerMax matches known object durations
     9→ *  3. AI decision interval is configured to 30s
    10→ *  4. Characters cycle through walk→interact→idle→walk
    11→ *
    12→ * Tests against the main page (which exposes window._gameEngine).
    13→ */
    14→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    15→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
    16→
    17→test.describe('M1: Activity Duration & 30s AI Cycle', () => {
    18→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    19→    await page.goto('/');
    20→    await waitForEngine(page);
    21→  });
    22→
    23→  // ── AI CYCLE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24→
    25→  test('AI decisionIntervalMs is configured to 30 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    26→    const intervalMs = await page.evaluate(() =>
    27→      window._gameEngine.aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs
    28→    );
    29→    expect(intervalMs, 'AI decision interval must be 30000ms (30s)').toBe(30_000);
    30→  });
    31→
    32→  test('AI timer object is initialized (keyed per character)', async ({ page }) => {
    33→    // _timers is an object keyed by character name; verify it exists as an object
    34→    const timersIsObject = await page.evaluate(() => {
    35→      const timers = window._gameEngine.aiEngine._timers;
    36→      return timers !== null && typeof timers === 'object' && !Array.isArray(timers);
    37→    });
    38→    expect(timersIsObject, 'aiEngine._timers should be an object').toBe(true);
    39→  });
    40→
    41→  test('second AI decision round fires after characters finish interacting', async ({ page }) => {
    42→    // This test does its own goto so the interceptor captures ALL requests from page load.
    43→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    44→
    45→    const requestTimestamps = [];
    46→    page.on('request', req => {
    47→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
    48→        requestTimestamps.push(Date.now());
    49→      }
    50→    });
    51→
    52→    // Navigate AFTER setting up the interceptor
    53→    await page.goto('/');
    54→    await waitForEngine(page);
    55→
    56→    // Wait for first batch (all 3 chars fire within ~5s of load)
    57→    await page.waitForTimeout(8_000);
    58→
    59→    const firstBatch = requestTimestamps.length;
    60→    expect(firstBatch, 'First batch: all 3 characters should have fired').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    61→
    62→    // Wait for characters to finish interacting (up to 60s) + 30s cycle interval
    63→    // Total: up to 90s real time for second batch
    64→    await page.waitForTimeout(90_000);
    65→
    66→    const totalRequests = requestTimestamps.length;
    67→    const secondBatch = totalRequests - firstBatch;
    68→    expect(secondBatch, 'Second AI decision batch should have fired for at least 1 character').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    69→
    70→    if (secondBatch >= 1) {
    71→      const lastFirst = requestTimestamps[firstBatch - 1];
    72→      const firstSecond = requestTimestamps[firstBatch];
    73→      const gapMs = firstSecond - lastFirst;
    74→      console.log(`AI cycle gap between rounds: ${(gapMs / 1000).toFixed(1)}s (interval = 30s)`);
    75→    }
    76→    console.log(`Total LLM requests in ~100s: ${totalRequests} (first: ${firstBatch}, second: ${secondBatch})`);
    77→  });
    78→
    79→  // ── ACTIVITY DURATION ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    80→
    81→  test('character stays in interacting state for expected duration', async ({ page }) => {
    82→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    83→
    84→    // Wait for a character to start interacting
    85→    const startState = await pollEngineState(
    86→      page,
    87→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),
    88→      20_000
    89→    );
    90→    const interactingChar = startState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'interacting');
    91→    expect(interactingChar, 'Expected at least one character to be interacting').toBeTruthy();
    92→
    93→    // Record entry time and check interactTimerMax
    94→    const timerInfo = await page.evaluate(name => {
    95→      const char = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === name);
    96→      return {
    97→        label: char.label,
    98→        interactTimer: char.interactTimer,
    99→        interactTimerMax: char.interactTimerMax,
   100→        action: char.action,
   101→      };
   102→    }, interactingChar.label);
   103→
   104→    console.log(`${timerInfo.label} interactTimerMax: ${timerInfo.interactTimerMax}ms`);
   105→    expect(timerInfo.interactTimerMax, 'interactTimerMax should be > 0').toBeGreaterThan(0);
   106→
   107→    // Verify character stays interacting for at least half the remaining duration
   108→    // (we don't wait the full duration to keep test fast)
   109→    const halfRemaining = Math.min((timerInfo.interactTimerMax - timerInfo.interactTimer) / 2, 8000);
   110→    await page.waitForTimeout(halfRemaining);
   111→
   112→    const midState = await page.evaluate(name => {
   113→      const char = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === name);
   114→      return { label: char.label, action: char.action };
   115→    }, interactingChar.label);
   116→
   117→    expect(midState.action, `${midState.label} should still be interacting mid-activity`)
   118→      .toBe('interacting');
   119→  });
   120→
   121→  test('interactTimerMax values fall within expected object duration ranges', async ({ page }) => {
   122→    test.setTimeout(60_000);
   123→
   124→    // Wait for at least one character to interact
   125→    await pollEngineState(
   126→      page,
   127→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),
   128→      20_000
   129→    );
   130→
   131→    const timerValues = await page.evaluate(() =>
   132→      window._gameEngine.characters
   133→        .filter(c => c.action === 'interacting')
   134→        .map(c => ({
   135→          label: c.label,
   136→          interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,
   137→        }))
   138→    );
   139→
   140→    for (const { label, interactTimerMax } of timerValues) {
   141→      // Known activity durations range from ~12s (bonsai) to ~60s (bed)
   142→      // All durations should be reasonable: 5s–120s
   143→      expect(interactTimerMax, `${label}: duration out of expected range (5s–120s)`)
   144→        .toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5_000);
   145→      expect(interactTimerMax, `${label}: duration out of expected range (5s–120s)`)
   146→        .toBeLessThanOrEqual(120_000);
   147→      console.log(`${label}: interactTimerMax = ${interactTimerMax / 1000}s`);
   148→    }
   149→  });
   150→
   151→  test('activity timer resets after completion (character cycles through activities)', async ({ page }) => {
   152→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
   153→
   154→    // Wait for a character to start interacting
   155→    await pollEngineState(
   156→      page,
   157→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),
   158→      20_000
   159→    );
   160→
   161→    // Find a character who is interacting with time remaining
   162→    const initial = await page.evaluate(() =>
   163→      window._gameEngine.characters
   164→        .filter(c => c.action === 'interacting')
   165→        .map(c => ({
   166→          label: c.label,
   167→          interactTimer: c.interactTimer,
   168→          interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,
   169→          remaining: c.interactTimerMax - c.interactTimer,
   170→        }))
   171→        .sort((a, b) => a.remaining - b.remaining)
   172→    );
   173→
   174→    if (initial.length === 0) {
   175→      console.log('No interacting characters found, skipping');
   176→      return;
   177→    }
   178→
   179→    const target = initial[0];
   180→    const waitMs = Math.min(target.remaining + 5000, 30_000);
   181→    console.log(`Tracking ${target.label}: ${target.remaining}ms remaining, waiting ${waitMs}ms...`);
   182→
   183→    await page.waitForTimeout(waitMs);
   184→
   185→    // After waiting, verify the character's timer has reset (interactTimer < original interactTimerMax)
   186→    // This confirms the activity completed and a new cycle began
   187→    const after = await page.evaluate(label => {
   188→      const c = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === label);
   189→      return {
   190→        label: c.label,
   191→        action: c.action,
   192→        interactTimer: c.interactTimer,
   193→        interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,
   194→      };
   195→    }, target.label);
   196→
   197→    console.log(`${target.label} after activity:`, JSON.stringify(after, null, 2));
   198→
   199→    // The timer must have moved — either:
   200→    // a) reset (new activity started: interactTimer < original timer value), OR
   201→    // b) character is idle/walking (left interacting state)
   202→    if (after.action === 'interacting') {
   203→      // If still interacting, the timer must have reset (new cycle)
   204→      expect(after.interactTimer, `${target.label}: activity timer should have reset`)
   205→        .toBeLessThan(target.interactTimer + 1000); // timer reset means lower than original
   206→    } else {
   207→      // Left interacting state — that's also valid
   208→      expect(['idle', 'walking']).toContain(after.action);
   209→    }
   210→  });
   211→
   212→  // ── ACTION TYPE COVERAGE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   213→
   214→  test('idle action type is observed on characters', async ({ page }) => {
   215→    // Engine starts with characters in idle (before first decision)
   216→    const initialState = await getEngineState(page);
   217→    const idleChars = initialState.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'idle');
   218→    // At start at least some characters should be idle or walking
   219→    const hasIdleOrWalking = initialState.characters.some(
   220→      c => c.action === 'idle' || c.action === 'walking'
   221→    );
   222→    expect(hasIdleOrWalking, 'Characters should start in idle or walking state').toBe(true);
   223→  });
   224→
   225→  test('walking action type is observed within 15 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
   226→    const state = await pollEngineState(
   227→      page,
   228→      s => s.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
   229→      15_000
   230→    );
   231→    expect(state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking')).toBe(true);
   232→  });
   233→
   234→  test('interacting action type is observed within 30 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
   235→    const state = await pollEngineState(
   236→      page,
   237→      s => s.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),
   238→      30_000
   239→    );
   240→    expect(state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting')).toBe(true);
   241→  });
   242→
   243→  test('LLM response action field contains only valid action types', async ({ page }) => {
   244→    const decisions = [];
   245→
   246→    page.on('response', async res => {
   247→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {
   248→        try {
   249→          const json = await res.json();
   250→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
   251→          if (text) decisions.push(JSON.parse(text));
   252→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   253→      }
   254→    });
   255→
   256→    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);
   257→
   258→    expect(decisions.length, 'No LLM decisions received').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
   259→    const validActions = ['move', 'interact', 'idle', 'talk'];
   260→    for (const d of decisions) {
   261→      expect(validActions, `Unexpected action: ${d.action}`).toContain(d.action);
   262→    }
   263→    const actionTypes = [...new Set(decisions.map(d => d.action))];
   264→    console.log('Action types seen in LLM responses:', actionTypes);
   265→  });
   266→});
   267→

window._gameEngine.aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs\n    28\u2192    );\n    29\u2192    expect(intervalMs, 'AI decision interval must be 30000ms (30s)').toBe(30_000);\n    30\u2192  });\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192  test('AI timer object is initialized (keyed per character)', async ({ page }) => {\n    33\u2192    // _timers is an object keyed by character name; verify it exists as an object\n    34\u2192    const timersIsObject = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    35\u2192      const timers = window._gameEngine.aiEngine._timers;\n    36\u2192      return timers !== null && typeof timers === 'object' && !Array.isArray(timers);\n    37\u2192    });\n    38\u2192    expect(timersIsObject, 'aiEngine._timers should be an object').toBe(true);\n    39\u2192  });\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192  test('second AI decision round fires after characters finish interacting', async ({ page }) => {\n    42\u2192    // This test does its own goto so the interceptor captures ALL requests from page load.\n    43\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192    const requestTimestamps = [];\n    46\u2192    page.on('request', req => {\n    47\u2192      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {\n    48\u2192        requestTimestamps.push(Date.now());\n    49\u2192      }\n    50\u2192    });\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192    // Navigate AFTER setting up the interceptor\n    53\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n    54\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192    // Wait for first batch (all 3 chars fire within ~5s of load)\n    57\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(8_000);\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192    const firstBatch = requestTimestamps.length;\n    60\u2192    expect(firstBatch, 'First batch: all 3 characters should have fired').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192    // Wait for characters to finish interacting (up to 60s) + 30s cycle interval\n    63\u2192    // Total: up to 90s real time for second batch\n    64\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(90_000);\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192    const totalRequests = requestTimestamps.length;\n    67\u2192    const secondBatch = totalRequests - firstBatch;\n    68\u2192    expect(secondBatch, 'Second AI decision batch should have fired for at least 1 character').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192    if (secondBatch >= 1) {\n    71\u2192      const lastFirst = requestTimestamps[firstBatch - 1];\n    72\u2192      const firstSecond = requestTimestamps[firstBatch];\n    73\u2192      const gapMs = firstSecond - lastFirst;\n    74\u2192      console.log(`AI cycle gap between rounds: ${(gapMs / 1000).toFixed(1)}s (interval = 30s)`);\n    75\u2192    }\n    76\u2192    console.log(`Total LLM requests in ~100s: ${totalRequests} (first: ${firstBatch}, second: ${secondBatch})`);\n    77\u2192  });\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 ACTIVITY DURATION \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192  test('character stays in interacting state for expected duration', async ({ page }) => {\n    82\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192    // Wait for a character to start interacting\n    85\u2192    const startState = await pollEngineState(\n    86\u2192      page,\n    87\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),\n    88\u2192      20_000\n    89\u2192    );\n    90\u2192    const interactingChar = startState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'interacting');\n    91\u2192    expect(interactingChar, 'Expected at least one character to be interacting').toBeTruthy();\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192    // Record entry time and check interactTimerMax\n    94\u2192    const timerInfo = await page.evaluate(name => {\n    95\u2192      const char = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === name);\n    96\u2192      return {\n    97\u2192        label: char.label,\n    98\u2192        interactTimer: char.interactTimer,\n    99\u2192        interactTimerMax: char.interactTimerMax,\n   100\u2192        action: char.action,\n   101\u2192      };\n   102\u2192    }, interactingChar.label);\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192    console.log(`${timerInfo.label} interactTimerMax: ${timerInfo.interactTimerMax}ms`);\n   105\u2192    expect(timerInfo.interactTimerMax, 'interactTimerMax should be > 0').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192    // Verify character stays interacting for at least half the remaining duration\n   108\u2192    // (we don't wait the full duration to keep test fast)\n   109\u2192    const halfRemaining = Math.min((timerInfo.interactTimerMax - timerInfo.interactTimer) / 2, 8000);\n   110\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(halfRemaining);\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192    const midState = await page.evaluate(name => {\n   113\u2192      const char = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === name);\n   114\u2192      return { label: char.label, action: char.action };\n   115\u2192    }, interactingChar.label);\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192    expect(midState.action, `${midState.label} should still be interacting mid-activity`)\n   118\u2192      .toBe('interacting');\n   119\u2192  });\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192  test('interactTimerMax values fall within expected object duration ranges', async ({ page }) => {\n   122\u2192    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192    // Wait for at least one character to interact\n   125\u2192    await pollEngineState(\n   126\u2192      page,\n   127\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),\n   128\u2192      20_000\n   129\u2192    );\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192    const timerValues = await page.evaluate(() =>\n   132\u2192      window._gameEngine.characters\n   133\u2192        .filter(c => c.action === 'interacting')\n   134\u2192        .map(c => ({\n   135\u2192          label: c.label,\n   136\u2192          interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,\n   137\u2192        }))\n   138\u2192    );\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192    for (const { label, interactTimerMax } of timerValues) {\n   141\u2192      // Known activity durations range from ~12s (bonsai) to ~60s (bed)\n   142\u2192      // All durations should be reasonable: 5s\u2013120s\n   143\u2192      expect(interactTimerMax, `${label}: duration out of expected range (5s\u2013120s)`)\n   144\u2192        .toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5_000);\n   145\u2192      expect(interactTimerMax, `${label}: duration out of expected range (5s\u2013120s)`)\n   146\u2192        .toBeLessThanOrEqual(120_000);\n   147\u2192      console.log(`${label}: interactTimerMax = ${interactTimerMax / 1000}s`);\n   148\u2192    }\n   149\u2192  });\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192  test('activity timer resets after completion (character cycles through activities)', async ({ page }) => {\n   152\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192    // Wait for a character to start interacting\n   155\u2192    await pollEngineState(\n   156\u2192      page,\n   157\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),\n   158\u2192      20_000\n   159\u2192    );\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192    // Find a character who is interacting with time remaining\n   162\u2192    const initial = await page.evaluate(() =>\n   163\u2192      window._gameEngine.characters\n   164\u2192        .filter(c => c.action === 'interacting')\n   165\u2192        .map(c => ({\n   166\u2192          label: c.label,\n   167\u2192          interactTimer: c.interactTimer,\n   168\u2192          interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,\n   169\u2192          remaining: c.interactTimerMax - c.interactTimer,\n   170\u2192        }))\n   171\u2192        .sort((a, b) => a.remaining - b.remaining)\n   172\u2192    );\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192    if (initial.length === 0) {\n   175\u2192      console.log('No interacting characters found, skipping');\n   176\u2192      return;\n   177\u2192    }\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192    const target = initial[0];\n   180\u2192    const waitMs = Math.min(target.remaining + 5000, 30_000);\n   181\u2192    console.log(`Tracking ${target.label}: ${target.remaining}ms remaining, waiting ${waitMs}ms...`);\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(waitMs);\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192    // After waiting, verify the character's timer has reset (interactTimer < original interactTimerMax)\n   186\u2192    // This confirms the activity completed and a new cycle began\n   187\u2192    const after = await page.evaluate(label => {\n   188\u2192      const c = window._gameEngine.characters.find(c => c.label === label);\n   189\u2192      return {\n   190\u2192        label: c.label,\n   191\u2192        action: c.action,\n   192\u2192        interactTimer: c.interactTimer,\n   193\u2192        interactTimerMax: c.interactTimerMax,\n   194\u2192      };\n   195\u2192    }, target.label);\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192    console.log(`${target.label} after activity:`, JSON.stringify(after, null, 2));\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192    // The timer must have moved \u2014 either:\n   200\u2192    // a) reset (new activity started: interactTimer < original timer value), OR\n   201\u2192    // b) character is idle/walking (left interacting state)\n   202\u2192    if (after.action === 'interacting') {\n   203\u2192      // If still interacting, the timer must have reset (new cycle)\n   204\u2192      expect(after.interactTimer, `${target.label}: activity timer should have reset`)\n   205\u2192        .toBeLessThan(target.interactTimer + 1000); // timer reset means lower than original\n   206\u2192    } else {\n   207\u2192      // Left interacting state \u2014 that's also valid\n   208\u2192      expect(['idle', 'walking']).toContain(after.action);\n   209\u2192    }\n   210\u2192  });\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 ACTION TYPE COVERAGE \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192  test('idle action type is observed on characters', async ({ page }) => {\n   215\u2192    // Engine starts with characters in idle (before first decision)\n   216\u2192    const initialState = await getEngineState(page);\n   217\u2192    const idleChars = initialState.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'idle');\n   218\u2192    // At start at least some characters should be idle or walking\n   219\u2192    const hasIdleOrWalking = initialState.characters.some(\n   220\u2192      c => c.action === 'idle' || c.action === 'walking'\n   221\u2192    );\n   222\u2192    expect(hasIdleOrWalking, 'Characters should start in idle or walking state').toBe(true);\n   223\u2192  });\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192  test('walking action type is observed within 15 seconds', async ({ page }) => {\n   226\u2192    const state = await pollEngineState(\n   227\u2192      page,\n   228\u2192      s => s.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n   229\u2192      15_000\n   230\u2192    );\n   231\u2192    expect(state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking')).toBe(true);\n   232\u2192  });\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192  test('interacting action type is observed within 30 seconds', async ({ page }) => {\n   235\u2192    const state = await pollEngineState(\n   236\u2192      page,\n   237\u2192      s => s.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),\n   238\u2192      30_000\n   239\u2192    );\n   240\u2192    expect(state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting')).toBe(true);\n   241\u2192  });\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192  test('LLM response action field contains only valid action types', async ({ page }) => {\n   244\u2192    const decisions = [];\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192    page.on('response', async res => {\n   247\u2192      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {\n   248\u2192        try {\n   249\u2192          const json = await res.json();\n   250\u2192          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;\n   251\u2192          if (text) decisions.push(JSON.parse(text));\n   252\u2192        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n   253\u2192      }\n   254\u2192    });\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192    expect(decisions.length, 'No LLM decisions received').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n   259\u2192    const validActions = ['move', 'interact', 'idle', 'talk'];\n   260\u2192    for (const d of decisions) {\n   261\u2192      expect(validActions, `Unexpected action: ${d.action}`).toContain(d.action);\n   262\u2192    }\n   263\u2192    const actionTypes = [...new Set(decisions.map(d => d.action))];\n   264\u2192    console.log('Action types seen in LLM responses:', actionTypes);\n   265\u2192  });\n   266\u2192});\n   267\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-234
     4→ * Smart Objects — Slot System
     5→ *
     6→ * Verifies the slot/capacity system described in the M1 spec:
     7→ *  - Every object has a #slots-{id} element with pip indicators
     8→ *  - Capacity-1 objects (most): 1 pip, shows "1/1 free"
     9→ *  - Multi-capacity objects (kotatsu, tv): 2 pips, shows "2/2 free"
    10→ *  - Slot state changes: available → reserved (character walking toward) → occupied (character using)
    11→ *  - When at capacity, label shows "full"
    12→ *
    13→ * Target: /debug.html
    14→ */
    15→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    16→
    17→async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    18→  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });
    19→  await page.waitForFunction(
    20→    () => {
    21→      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]');
    22→      return cards.length >= 3 && cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');
    23→    },
    24→    { timeout }
    25→  );
    26→}
    27→
    28→test.describe('M1: Smart Objects — Slot System', () => {
    29→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    30→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
    31→    await waitForDashboard(page);
    32→  });
    33→
    34→  // ── CAPACITY DISPLAY ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    35→
    36→  test('all 10 objects have a slot-info element in the debug UI', async ({ page }) => {
    37→    const objectIds = [
    38→      'tea_set', 'kotatsu', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge',
    39→      'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel', 'tv',
    40→    ];
    41→    for (const id of objectIds) {
    42→      const slotEl = page.locator(`#slots-${id}`);
    43→      await expect(slotEl, `#slots-${id} should be visible`).toBeVisible();
    44→    }
    45→  });
    46→
    47→  test('all 10 objects show pip indicators', async ({ page }) => {
    48→    const objectIds = [
    49→      'tea_set', 'kotatsu', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge',
    50→      'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel', 'tv',
    51→    ];
    52→    for (const id of objectIds) {
    53→      const pips = page.locator(`#slots-${id} .slot-pip`);
    54→      const count = await pips.count();
    55→      expect(count, `${id}: expected at least 1 pip indicator`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    56→    }
    57→  });
    58→
    59→  test('capacity-1 objects show 1 pip each', async ({ page }) => {
    60→    const singleCapObjects = ['tea_set', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge', 'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel'];
    61→    for (const id of singleCapObjects) {
    62→      const pips = page.locator(`#slots-${id} .slot-pip`);
    63→      const count = await pips.count();
    64→      expect(count, `${id}: expected 1 pip (capacity=1)`).toBe(1);
    65→    }
    66→  });
    67→
    68→  test('kotatsu has 2 pips (capacity=2)', async ({ page }) => {
    69→    const pips = page.locator('#slots-kotatsu .slot-pip');
    70→    const count = await pips.count();
    71→    expect(count, 'kotatsu: expected 2 pips (capacity=2)').toBe(2);
    72→  });
    73→
    74→  test('tv has 2 pips (capacity=2)', async ({ page }) => {
    75→    const pips = page.locator('#slots-tv .slot-pip');
    76→    const count = await pips.count();
    77→    expect(count, 'tv: expected 2 pips (capacity=2)').toBe(2);
    78→  });
    79→
    80→  test('slot labels show "N/N free" format for available objects', async ({ page }) => {
    81→    // At page load most objects should be free
    82→    const labels = await page.evaluate(() => {
    83→      const ids = ['kotatsu', 'stove', 'fridge', 'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel', 'tv'];
    84→      return ids.map(id => {
    85→        const el = document.querySelector(`#slots-${id} .slot-label`);
    86→        return { id, text: el ? el.textContent.trim() : null };
    87→      });
    88→    });
    89→
    90→    for (const { id, text } of labels) {
    91→      expect(text, `${id}: slot label should exist`).toBeTruthy();
    92→      // Acceptable: "N/N free" OR "full" (if already taken at load time)
    93→      const isValidLabel = /^\d+\/\d+ free$/.test(text) || text === 'full';
    94→      expect(isValidLabel, `${id}: unexpected slot label "${text}"`).toBe(true);
    95→    }
    96→    console.log('Slot labels:', labels.map(l => `${l.id}: ${l.text}`).join(', '));
    97→  });
    98→
    99→  test('kotatsu slot label shows "2/2 free" when unoccupied', async ({ page }) => {
   100→    // Verify the kotatsu starts with both slots available
   101→    const label = await page.locator('#slots-kotatsu .slot-label').innerText();
   102→    // It may already be reserved/occupied, so just verify it is a valid format
   103→    expect(label).toMatch(/^(\d\/\d free|full)$/);
   104→    console.log('Kotatsu slot label:', label);
   105→  });
   106→
   107→  // ── SLOT STATE CHANGES ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   108→
   109→  test('slot pip changes to reserved when a character walks toward the object', async ({ page }) => {
   110→    test.setTimeout(90_000);
   111→
   112→    // Poll until at least one object has a reserved pip
   113→    await page.waitForFunction(
   114→      () => document.querySelectorAll('.slot-pip.reserved').length > 0,
   115→      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }
   116→    );
   117→
   118→    const reservedInfo = await page.evaluate(() => {
   119→      const pips = document.querySelectorAll('.slot-pip.reserved');
   120→      return Array.from(pips).map(pip => ({
   121→        title: pip.getAttribute('title'),
   122→        parentId: pip.closest('[id^="slots-"]')?.id,
   123→      }));
   124→    });
   125→
   126→    expect(reservedInfo.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   127→    // Title should include "heading here" text
   128→    for (const info of reservedInfo) {
   129→      expect(info.title, 'Reserved pip should have character info in title').toMatch(/heading here/);
   130→    }
   131→    console.log('Reserved slot pips:', reservedInfo);
   132→  });
   133→
   134→  test('object shows "full" label when all slots are occupied or reserved', async ({ page }) => {
   135→    test.setTimeout(90_000);
   136→
   137→    // Poll until at least one object shows "full" in slot label
   138→    await page.waitForFunction(
   139→      () => {
   140→        const labels = document.querySelectorAll('.slot-label.full');
   141→        return labels.length > 0;
   142→      },
   143→      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }
   144→    );
   145→
   146→    const fullObjects = await page.evaluate(() => {
   147→      const labels = document.querySelectorAll('.slot-label.full');
   148→      return Array.from(labels).map(el => ({
   149→        slotId: el.closest('[id^="slots-"]')?.id,
   150→        label: el.textContent.trim(),
   151→      }));
   152→    });
   153→
   154→    expect(fullObjects.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   155→    for (const obj of fullObjects) {
   156→      expect(obj.label).toBe('full');
   157→    }
   158→    console.log('Full objects:', fullObjects);
   159→  });
   160→
   161→  // ── BLOCKING BEHAVIOR ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   162→
   163→  test('single-capacity object pip becomes reserved when character heads there', async ({ page }) => {
   164→    test.setTimeout(60_000);
   165→
   166→    // Single-capacity object IDs
   167→    const singleCapIds = ['tea_set', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge', 'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel'];
   168→
   169→    // Wait for any single-capacity object to be reserved
   170→    await page.waitForFunction(
   171→      (ids) => ids.some(id => {
   172→        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);
   173→        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;
   174→      }),
   175→      singleCapIds,
   176→      { timeout: 45_000, polling: 500 }
   177→    );
   178→
   179→    const reservedSingle = await page.evaluate((ids) =>
   180→      ids.filter(id => {
   181→        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);
   182→        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;
   183→      }),
   184→      singleCapIds
   185→    );
   186→
   187→    expect(reservedSingle.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   188→    console.log('Single-cap objects with reserved slot:', reservedSingle);
   189→  });
   190→});
   191→

    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-234\n     4\u2192 * Smart Objects \u2014 Slot System\n     5\u2192 *\n     6\u2192 * Verifies the slot/capacity system described in the M1 spec:\n     7\u2192 *  - Every object has a #slots-{id} element with pip indicators\n     8\u2192 *  - Capacity-1 objects (most): 1 pip, shows \"1/1 free\"\n     9\u2192 *  - Multi-capacity objects (kotatsu, tv): 2 pips, shows \"2/2 free\"\n    10\u2192 *  - Slot state changes: available \u2192 reserved (character walking toward) \u2192 occupied (character using)\n    11\u2192 *  - When at capacity, label shows \"full\"\n    12\u2192 *\n    13\u2192 * Target: /debug.html\n    14\u2192 */\n    15\u2192const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n    18\u2192  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });\n    19\u2192  await page.waitForFunction(\n    20\u2192    () => {\n    21\u2192      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n    22\u2192      return cards.length >= 3 && cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');\n    23\u2192    },\n    24\u2192    { timeout }\n    25\u2192  );\n    26\u2192}\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192test.describe('M1: Smart Objects \u2014 Slot System', () => {\n    29\u2192  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n    30\u2192    await page.goto('/debug.html');\n    31\u2192    await waitForDashboard(page);\n    32\u2192  });\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 CAPACITY DISPLAY \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192  test('all 10 objects have a slot-info element in the debug UI', async ({ page }) => {\n    37\u2192    const objectIds = [\n    38\u2192      'tea_set', 'kotatsu', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge',\n    39\u2192      'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel', 'tv',\n    40\u2192    ];\n    41\u2192    for (const id of objectIds) {\n    42\u2192      const slotEl = page.locator(`#slots-${id}`);\n    43\u2192      await expect(slotEl, `#slots-${id} should be visible`).toBeVisible();\n    44\u2192    }\n    45\u2192  });\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192  test('all 10 objects show pip indicators', async ({ page }) => {\n    48\u2192    const objectIds = [\n    49\u2192      'tea_set', 'kotatsu', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge',\n    50\u2192      'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel', 'tv',\n    51\u2192    ];\n    52\u2192    for (const id of objectIds) {\n    53\u2192      const pips = page.locator(`#slots-${id} .slot-pip`);\n    54\u2192      const count = await pips.count();\n    55\u2192      expect(count, `${id}: expected at least 1 pip indicator`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n    56\u2192    }\n    57\u2192  });\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192  test('capacity-1 objects show 1 pip each', async ({ page }) => {\n    60\u2192    const singleCapObjects = ['tea_set', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge', 'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel'];\n    61\u2192    for (const id of singleCapObjects) {\n    62\u2192      const pips = page.locator(`#slots-${id} .slot-pip`);\n    63\u2192      const count = await pips.count();\n    64\u2192      expect(count, `${id}: expected 1 pip (capacity=1)`).toBe(1);\n    65\u2192    }\n    66\u2192  });\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192  test('kotatsu has 2 pips (capacity=2)', async ({ page }) => {\n    69\u2192    const pips = page.locator('#slots-kotatsu .slot-pip');\n    70\u2192    const count = await pips.count();\n    71\u2192    expect(count, 'kotatsu: expected 2 pips (capacity=2)').toBe(2);\n    72\u2192  });\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192  test('tv has 2 pips (capacity=2)', async ({ page }) => {\n    75\u2192    const pips = page.locator('#slots-tv .slot-pip');\n    76\u2192    const count = await pips.count();\n    77\u2192    expect(count, 'tv: expected 2 pips (capacity=2)').toBe(2);\n    78\u2192  });\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192  test('slot labels show \"N/N free\" format for available objects', async ({ page }) => {\n    81\u2192    // At page load most objects should be free\n    82\u2192    const labels = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    83\u2192      const ids = ['kotatsu', 'stove', 'fridge', 'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel', 'tv'];\n    84\u2192      return ids.map(id => {\n    85\u2192        const el = document.querySelector(`#slots-${id} .slot-label`);\n    86\u2192        return { id, text: el ? el.textContent.trim() : null };\n    87\u2192      });\n    88\u2192    });\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192    for (const { id, text } of labels) {\n    91\u2192      expect(text, `${id}: slot label should exist`).toBeTruthy();\n    92\u2192      // Acceptable: \"N/N free\" OR \"full\" (if already taken at load time)\n    93\u2192      const isValidLabel = /^\\d+\\/\\d+ free$/.test(text) || text === 'full';\n    94\u2192      expect(isValidLabel, `${id}: unexpected slot label \"${text}\"`).toBe(true);\n    95\u2192    }\n    96\u2192    console.log('Slot labels:', labels.map(l => `${l.id}: ${l.text}`).join(', '));\n    97\u2192  });\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192  test('kotatsu slot label shows \"2/2 free\" when unoccupied', async ({ page }) => {\n   100\u2192    // Verify the kotatsu starts with both slots available\n   101\u2192    const label = await page.locator('#slots-kotatsu .slot-label').innerText();\n   102\u2192    // It may already be reserved/occupied, so just verify it is a valid format\n   103\u2192    expect(label).toMatch(/^(\\d\\/\\d free|full)$/);\n   104\u2192    console.log('Kotatsu slot label:', label);\n   105\u2192  });\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 SLOT STATE CHANGES \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192  test('slot pip changes to reserved when a character walks toward the object', async ({ page }) => {\n   110\u2192    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192    // Poll until at least one object has a reserved pip\n   113\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   114\u2192      () => document.querySelectorAll('.slot-pip.reserved').length > 0,\n   115\u2192      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }\n   116\u2192    );\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    const reservedInfo = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   119\u2192      const pips = document.querySelectorAll('.slot-pip.reserved');\n   120\u2192      return Array.from(pips).map(pip => ({\n   121\u2192        title: pip.getAttribute('title'),\n   122\u2192        parentId: pip.closest('[id^=\"slots-\"]')?.id,\n   123\u2192      }));\n   124\u2192    });\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192    expect(reservedInfo.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   127\u2192    // Title should include \"heading here\" text\n   128\u2192    for (const info of reservedInfo) {\n   129\u2192      expect(info.title, 'Reserved pip should have character info in title').toMatch(/heading here/);\n   130\u2192    }\n   131\u2192    console.log('Reserved slot pips:', reservedInfo);\n   132\u2192  });\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192  test('object shows \"full\" label when all slots are occupied or reserved', async ({ page }) => {\n   135\u2192    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192    // Poll until at least one object shows \"full\" in slot label\n   138\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   139\u2192      () => {\n   140\u2192        const labels = document.querySelectorAll('.slot-label.full');\n   141\u2192        return labels.length > 0;\n   142\u2192      },\n   143\u2192      { timeout: 75_000, polling: 1000 }\n   144\u2192    );\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192    const fullObjects = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   147\u2192      const labels = document.querySelectorAll('.slot-label.full');\n   148\u2192      return Array.from(labels).map(el => ({\n   149\u2192        slotId: el.closest('[id^=\"slots-\"]')?.id,\n   150\u2192        label: el.textContent.trim(),\n   151\u2192      }));\n   152\u2192    });\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192    expect(fullObjects.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   155\u2192    for (const obj of fullObjects) {\n   156\u2192      expect(obj.label).toBe('full');\n   157\u2192    }\n   158\u2192    console.log('Full objects:', fullObjects);\n   159\u2192  });\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 BLOCKING BEHAVIOR \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192  test('single-capacity object pip becomes reserved when character heads there', async ({ page }) => {\n   164\u2192    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192    // Single-capacity object IDs\n   167\u2192    const singleCapIds = ['tea_set', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge', 'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel'];\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192    // Wait for any single-capacity object to be reserved\n   170\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   171\u2192      (ids) => ids.some(id => {\n   172\u2192        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n   173\u2192        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;\n   174\u2192      }),\n   175\u2192      singleCapIds,\n   176\u2192      { timeout: 45_000, polling: 500 }\n   177\u2192    );\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192    const reservedSingle = await page.evaluate((ids) =>\n   180\u2192      ids.filter(id => {\n   181\u2192        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n   182\u2192        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;\n   183\u2192      }),\n   184\u2192      singleCapIds\n   185\u2192    );\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192    expect(reservedSingle.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   188\u2192    console.log('Single-cap objects with reserved slot:', reservedSingle);\n   189\u2192  });\n   190\u2192});\n   191\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code.      1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-234
     4→ * Talk Handshaking
     5→ *
     6→ * Verifies the character-to-character conversation system:
     7→ *  - Characters can initiate talk via AI action: 'talk'
     8→ *  - On accept: both enter talking state, TALK event appears in event log
     9→ *  - On reject: REJECT event appears in event log with reason
    10→ *  - canTalk filter: LLM receives canTalk status for nearby characters
    11→ *  - Talk targets observed across all characters over time
    12→ *
    13→ * Target: /debug.html (for event log) and / (for engine state)
    14→ */
    15→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    16→const { waitForEngine, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
    17→
    18→async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    19→  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });
    20→  await page.waitForFunction(
    21→    () => {
    22→      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]');
    23→      return cards.length >= 3 && cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');
    24→    },
    25→    { timeout }
    26→  );
    27→}
    28→
    29→test.describe('M1: Talk Handshaking', () => {
    30→  // ── TALK EVENTS IN EVENT LOG ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    31→
    32→  test('TALK event appears in event log within 90 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    33→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    34→
    35→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
    36→    await waitForDashboard(page);
    37→
    38→    // Enable 4x speed to accelerate AI cycles
    39→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();
    40→
    41→    await page.waitForFunction(
    42→      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.talk') !== null,
    43→      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }
    44→    );
    45→
    46→    const talkEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {
    47→      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.talk');
    48→      return Array.from(entries).map(e => ({
    49→        obj: e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim(),
    50→        transition: e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),
    51→        tag: e.querySelector('.log-tag')?.textContent.trim(),
    52→      }));
    53→    });
    54→
    55→    expect(talkEntries.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    56→    for (const entry of talkEntries) {
    57→      expect(entry.tag).toBe('TALK');
    58→      // Transition should show "talking ↔ CharacterName"
    59→      expect(entry.transition).toMatch(/talking\s*↔\s*(Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);
    60→      // obj should be a character name
    61→      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(entry.obj);
    62→    }
    63→    console.log('TALK events found:', talkEntries);
    64→  });
    65→
    66→  test('AI decision with action=talk appears in event log', async ({ page }) => {
    67→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    68→
    69→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
    70→    await waitForDashboard(page);
    71→
    72→    // Enable 4x speed
    73→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();
    74→
    75→    // Wait for an AI event with "talk →" transition
    76→    await page.waitForFunction(
    77→      () => {
    78→        const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.ai');
    79→        return Array.from(entries).some(e => {
    80→          const t = e.querySelector('.log-transition');
    81→          return t && t.textContent.includes('talk →');
    82→        });
    83→      },
    84→      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }
    85→    );
    86→
    87→    const talkAiEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {
    88→      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.ai');
    89→      return Array.from(entries)
    90→        .filter(e => e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.includes('talk →'))
    91→        .map(e => ({
    92→          obj: e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim(),
    93→          transition: e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),
    94→        }));
    95→    });
    96→
    97→    expect(talkAiEntries.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    98→    for (const entry of talkAiEntries) {
    99→      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(entry.obj);
   100→      expect(entry.transition).toMatch(/talk → (Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);
   101→    }
   102→    console.log('AI talk decisions:', talkAiEntries);
   103→  });
   104→
   105→  // ── REJECT EVENTS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   106→
   107→  test('REJECT event appears in event log when character is busy', async ({ page }) => {
   108→    test.setTimeout(180_000);
   109→
   110→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
   111→    await waitForDashboard(page);
   112→
   113→    // Enable 4x speed to see more AI cycles
   114→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();
   115→
   116→    // REJECT events are probabilistic — they only fire when someone tries to talk
   117→    // to a character who is interacting or already talking.
   118→    // Wait up to 2.5 minutes (covers ~5 AI cycles at 30s each)
   119→    const found = await page.waitForFunction(
   120→      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.reject') !== null,
   121→      { timeout: 150_000, polling: 1000 }
   122→    ).catch(() => null);
   123→
   124→    if (!found) {
   125→      // REJECT events only happen when characters happen to conflict.
   126→      // Log a note but don't fail — this is probabilistic, not a product bug.
   127→      console.log('[SKIP] No REJECT events observed in this run (probabilistic — requires talk attempt on busy character)');
   128→      return;
   129→    }
   130→
   131→    const rejectEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {
   132→      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.reject');
   133→      return Array.from(entries).map(e => ({
   134→        obj: e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim(),
   135→        transition: e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),
   136→        tag: e.querySelector('.log-tag')?.textContent.trim(),
   137→      }));
   138→    });
   139→
   140→    expect(rejectEntries.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   141→    for (const entry of rejectEntries) {
   142→      expect(entry.tag).toBe('REJECT');
   143→      // Transition shows "→ CharacterName rejected (reason)"
   144→      expect(entry.transition).toMatch(/→\s*(Maple|Sol|Nyx)\s+rejected/);
   145→    }
   146→    console.log('REJECT events found:', rejectEntries);
   147→  });
   148→
   149→  // ── CAN-TALK FILTER ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   150→
   151→  test('LLM request body includes canTalk status for nearby characters', async ({ page }) => {
   152→    const requestBodies = [];
   153→
   154→    page.on('request', req => {
   155→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
   156→        try {
   157→          requestBodies.push(JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}'));
   158→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   159→      }
   160→    });
   161→
   162→    await page.goto('/');
   163→    await page.waitForFunction(
   164→      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,
   165→      { timeout: 20_000 }
   166→    );
   167→    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);
   168→
   169→    // At least some requests should include character info with canTalk
   170→    const withCanTalk = requestBodies.filter(body => {
   171→      const content = body.messages?.[0]?.content || '';
   172→      return content.includes('canTalk');
   173→    });
   174→
   175→    if (withCanTalk.length === 0) {
   176→      // canTalk may be in the system prompt instead of the user message
   177→      const withNearbyChars = requestBodies.filter(body => {
   178→        const sys = body.system || '';
   179→        const user = body.messages?.[0]?.content || '';
   180→        return sys.includes('canTalk') || user.includes('Nearby characters') || user.includes('characters');
   181→      });
   182→      expect(withNearbyChars.length, 'LLM requests should include character availability info').toBeGreaterThan(0);
   183→      console.log('Character info in LLM requests (system or user message):', withNearbyChars.length);
   184→    } else {
   185→      expect(withCanTalk.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   186→      console.log('LLM requests with canTalk field:', withCanTalk.length);
   187→    }
   188→  });
   189→
   190→  // ── CHARACTERS ENTER TALKING STATE ────────────────────────────────────────
   191→
   192→  test('characters enter talking action state during conversation', async ({ page }) => {
   193→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
   194→
   195→    await page.goto('/');
   196→    await page.waitForFunction(
   197→      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,
   198→      { timeout: 20_000 }
   199→    );
   200→
   201→    // Poll engine state for any character in talking state
   202→    const talkState = await pollEngineState(
   203→      page,
   204→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'talking'),
   205→      90_000,
   206→      500
   207→    ).catch(() => null);
   208→
   209→    if (!talkState) {
   210→      console.log('[SKIP] No talking state observed in this run (AI may not have chosen talk action)');
   211→      return;
   212→    }
   213→
   214→    const talkers = talkState.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'talking');
   215→    expect(talkers.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
   216→    console.log('Characters in talking state:', talkers.map(c => c.label).join(', '));
   217→  });
   218→
   219→  // ── TALK TARGETS DISTRIBUTED ACROSS CHARACTERS ───────────────────────────
   220→
   221→  test('talk action targets distributed across multiple characters over time', async ({ page }) => {
   222→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
   223→
   224→    const talkTargets = new Set();
   225→
   226→    page.on('response', async res => {
   227→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {
   228→        try {
   229→          const json = await res.json();
   230→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
   231→          if (text) {
   232→            const d = JSON.parse(text);
   233→            if (d.action === 'talk' && d.targetCharacter) {
   234→              talkTargets.add(d.targetCharacter);
   235→            }
   236→          }
   237→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   238→      }
   239→    });
   240→
   241→    await page.goto('/');
   242→    await page.waitForFunction(
   243→      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,
   244→      { timeout: 20_000 }
   245→    );
   246→    await page.waitForTimeout(90_000);
   247→
   248→    if (talkTargets.size === 0) {
   249→      console.log('[INFO] No talk actions observed in this run — AI chose other actions');
   250→      // Not a failure: AI is non-deterministic. The LLM responses valid action test already covers this.
   251→      return;
   252→    }
   253→
   254→    // If talk actions were observed, targets should be valid character names
   255→    for (const target of talkTargets) {
   256→      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(target);
   257→    }
   258→    console.log('Talk targets observed:', [...talkTargets]);
   259→  });
   260→});
   261→

log\n     9\u2192 *  - On reject: REJECT event appears in event log with reason\n    10\u2192 *  - canTalk filter: LLM receives canTalk status for nearby characters\n    11\u2192 *  - Talk targets observed across all characters over time\n    12\u2192 *\n    13\u2192 * Target: /debug.html (for event log) and / (for engine state)\n    14\u2192 */\n    15\u2192const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n    16\u2192const { waitForEngine, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n );\n    27\u2192}\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192test.describe('M1: Talk Handshaking', () => {\n    30\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TALK EVENTS IN EVENT LOG \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192  test('TALK event appears in event log within 90 seconds', async ({ page }) => {\n    33\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192    await page.goto('/debug.html');\n    36\u2192    await waitForDashboard(page);\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192    // Enable 4x speed to accelerate AI cycles\n    39\u2192    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n    42\u2192      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.talk') !== null,\n    43\u2192      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }\n    44\u2192    );\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192    const talkEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    47\u2192      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.talk');\n e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),\n    51\u2192        tag: e.querySelector('.log-tag')?.textContent.trim(),\n    52\u2192      }));\n    53\u2192    });\n    54\u2192\n 61\u2192      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(entry.obj);\n    62\u2192    }\n    63\u2192    console.log('TALK events found:', talkEntries);\n    64\u2192  });\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192  test('AI decision with action=talk appears in event log', async ({ page }) => {\n    67\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192    await page.goto('/debug.html');\n    70\u2192    await waitForDashboard(page);\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192    // Enable 4x speed\n    73\u2192    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192    // Wait for an AI event with \"talk \u2192\" transition\n    76\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n    77\u2192      () => {\n    78\u2192        const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.ai');\n   82\u2192        });\n    83\u2192      },\n    84\u2192      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }\n    85\u2192    );\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192    const talkAiEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    88\u2192      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.ai');\n    89\u2192      return Array.from(entries)\n    90\u2192        .filter(e =>   106\u2192\n   107\u2192  test('REJECT event appears in event log when character is busy', async ({ page }) => {\n   108\u2192    test.setTimeout(180_000);\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192    await page.goto('/debug.html');\n   111\u2192    await waitForDashboard(page);\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192    // Enable 4x speed to see more AI cycles\n   114\u2192    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    // REJECT events are probabilistic \u2014 they only fire when someone tries to talk\n   117\u2192    // to a character who is interacting or already talking.\n   118\u2192    // Wait up to 2.5 minutes (covers ~5 AI cycles at 30s each)\n   119\u2192    const found = await page.waitForFunction(\n   120\u2192      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.reject') !== null,\n   121\u2192      { timeout: 150_000, polling: 1000 }\n   122\u2192    ).catch(() => null);\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192    if (!found) {\n   125\u2192      // REJECT events only happen when characters happen to conflict.\n   126\u2192      // Log a note but don't fail \u2014 this is probabilistic, not a product bug.\n   127\u2192      console.log('[SKIP] No REJECT events observed in this run (probabilistic \u2014 requires talk attempt on busy character)');\n   128\u2192      return;\n   129\u2192    }\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192    const rejectEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   132\u2192      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.reject');\n   133\u2192      return Array.from(entries).map(e => ({\n   134\u2192        obj: e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim(),\n   135\u2192        transition: e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),\n   136\u2192        tag: e.querySelector('.log-tag')?.textContent.trim(),\n   137\u2192      }));\n   138\u2192    });\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192    expect(rejectEntries.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   141\u2192    for (const entry of rejectEntries) {\n   142\u2192      expect(entry.tag).toBe('REJECT');\n   143\u2192      // Transition shows \"\u2192 CharacterName rejected (reason)\"\n   144\u2192      expect(entry.transition).toMatch(/\u2192\\s*(Maple|Sol|Nyx)\\s+rejected/);\n   145\u2192    }\n   146\u2192    console.log('REJECT events found:', rejectEntries);\n   147\u2192  });\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 CAN-TALK FILTER \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192  test('LLM request body includes canTalk status for nearby characters', async ({ page }) => {\n   152\u2192    const requestBodies = [];\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192    page.on('request', req => {\n   155\u2192      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {\n   156\u2192        try {\n   157\u2192          requestBodies.push(JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}'));\n   158\u2192        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n   159\u2192      }\n   160\u2192    });\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   163\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   164\u2192      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n   165\u2192      { timeout: 20_000 }\n   166\u2192    );\n   167\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192    // At least some requests should include character info with canTalk\n   170\u2192    const withCanTalk = requestBodies.filter(body => {\n   171\u2192      const content = body.messages?.[0]?.content || '';\n   172\u2192      return content.includes('canTalk');\n   173\u2192    });\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192    if (withCanTalk.length === 0) {\n   176\u2192      // canTalk may be in the system prompt instead of the user message\n   177\u2192      const withNearbyChars = requestBodies.filter(body => {\n   178\u2192        const sys = body.system || '';\n   179\u2192        const user = body.messages?.[0]?.content || '';\n   180\u2192        return sys.includes('canTalk') || user.includes('Nearby characters') || user.includes('characters');\n   181\u2192      });\n   182\u2192      expect(withNearbyChars.length, 'LLM requests should include character availability info').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   183\u2192      console.log('Character info in LLM requests (system or user message):', withNearbyChars.length);\n   184\u2192    } else {\n   185\u2192      expect(withCanTalk.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   186\u2192      console.log('LLM requests with canTalk field:', withCanTalk.length);\n   187\u2192    }\n   188\u2192  });\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 CHARACTERS ENTER TALKING STATE \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192  test('characters enter talking action state during conversation', async ({ page }) => {\n   193\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   196\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   197\u2192      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n   198\u2192      { timeout: 20_000 }\n   199\u2192    );\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192    // Poll engine state for any character in talking state\n   202\u2192    const talkState = await pollEngineState(\n   203\u2192      page,\n   204\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'talking'),\n   205\u2192      90_000,\n   206\u2192      500\n   207\u2192    ).catch(() => null);\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192    if (!talkState) {\n   210\u2192      console.log('[SKIP] No talking state observed in this run (AI may not have chosen talk action)');\n   211\u2192      return;\n   212\u2192    }\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192    const talkers = talkState.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'talking');\n   215\u2192    expect(talkers.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n   216\u2192    console.log('Characters in talking state:', talkers.map(c => c.label).join(', '));\n   217\u2192  });\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TALK TARGETS DISTRIBUTED ACROSS CHARACTERS \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192  test('talk action targets distributed across multiple characters over time', async ({ page }) => {\n   222\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192    const talkTargets = new Set();\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192    page.on('response', async res => {\n   227\u2192      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {\n   228\u2192        try {\n   229\u2192          const json = await res.json();\n   230\u2192          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;\n   231\u2192          if (text) {\n   232\u2192            const d = JSON.parse(text);\n   233\u2192            if (d.action === 'talk' && d.targetCharacter) {\n   234\u2192              talkTargets.add(d.targetCharacter);\n   235\u2192            }\n   236\u2192          }\n   237\u2192        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n   238\u2192      }\n   239\u2192    });\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   242\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n   243\u2192      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,\n   244\u2192      { timeout: 20_000 }\n   245\u2192    );\n   246\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(90_000);\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192    if (talkTargets.size === 0) {\n   249\u2192      console.log('[INFO] No talk actions observed in this run \u2014 AI chose other actions');\n   250\u2192      // Not a failure: AI is non-deterministic. The LLM responses valid action test already covers this.\n   251\u2192      return;\n   252\u2192    }\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192    // If talk actions were observed, targets should be valid character names\n   255\u2192    for (const target of talkTargets) {\n   256\u2192      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(target);\n   257\u2192    }\n   258\u2192    console.log('Talk targets observed:', [...talkTargets]);\n   259\u2192  });\n   260\u2192});\n   261\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
