SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-234
     4→ * Fallback Logic
     5→ *
     6→ * Verifies the fallback system described in the M1 spec:
     7→ *  - FALLBACK "plan changed" fires when AI updates a character's plan mid-walk
     8→ *  - FALLBACK "unavailable" fires when a character's target object is taken
     9→ *  - Characters pick an alternative object or go idle on fallback
    10→ *  - Object reservations are released when a character's plan changes
    11→ *
    12→ * Target: /debug.html (event log shows FALLBACK entries)
    13→ */
    14→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    15→
    16→async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    17→  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });
    18→  await page.waitForFunction(
    19→    () => {
    20→      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]');
    21→      return cards.length >= 3 && cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');
    22→    },
    23→    { timeout }
    24→  );
    25→}
    26→
    27→test.describe('M1: Fallback Logic', () => {
    28→  // ── FALLBACK EVENTS IN EVENT LOG ──────────────────────────────────────────
    29→
    30→  test('FALLBACK event appears in event log within 90 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    31→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    32→
    33→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
    34→    await waitForDashboard(page);
    35→
    36→    // Enable 4x speed to see more AI cycles faster
    37→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();
    38→
    39→    await page.waitForFunction(
    40→      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.fallback') !== null,
    41→      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }
    42→    );
    43→
    44→    const fallbackEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {
    45→      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.fallback');
    46→      return Array.from(entries).map(e => ({
    47→        obj: e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim(),
    48→        transition: e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),
    49→        tag: e.querySelector('.log-tag')?.textContent.trim(),
    50→      }));
    51→    });
    52→
    53→    expect(fallbackEntries.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    54→    for (const entry of fallbackEntries) {
    55→      expect(entry.tag).toBe('FALLBACK');
    56→      // Transition shows "objectId plan changed → newTarget" or "objectId unavailable"
    57→      const isPlanChange = /plan changed/.test(entry.transition);
    58→      const isUnavailable = /unavailable/.test(entry.transition);
    59→      expect(isPlanChange || isUnavailable, `FALLBACK transition should mention "plan changed" or "unavailable": got "${entry.transition}"`).toBe(true);
    60→      // obj should be a character name
    61→      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(entry.obj);
    62→    }
    63→    console.log('FALLBACK events found:', fallbackEntries);
    64→  });
    65→
    66→  test('FALLBACK "plan changed" event shows old and new target', async ({ page }) => {
    67→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    68→
    69→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
    70→    await waitForDashboard(page);
    71→
    72→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();
    73→
    74→    // Wait for a "plan changed" fallback
    75→    const found = await page.waitForFunction(
    76→      () => {
    77→        const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.fallback');
    78→        return Array.from(entries).some(e => e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.includes('plan changed'));
    79→      },
    80→      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }
    81→    ).catch(() => null);
    82→
    83→    if (!found) {
    84→      console.log('[SKIP] No "plan changed" FALLBACK observed in this run (AI may not have changed plans mid-walk)');
    85→      return;
    86→    }
    87→
    88→    const planChangeFallbacks = await page.evaluate(() => {
    89→      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.fallback');
    90→      return Array.from(entries)
    91→        .filter(e => e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.includes('plan changed'))
    92→        .map(e => ({
    93→          obj: e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim(),
    94→          transition: e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),
    95→        }));
    96→    });
    97→
    98→    expect(planChangeFallbacks.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    99→    for (const entry of planChangeFallbacks) {
   100→      // Format: "oldTarget plan changed → newTarget" or "oldTarget plan changed"
   101→      expect(entry.transition).toMatch(/plan changed/);
   102→    }
   103→    console.log('Plan-change fallbacks:', planChangeFallbacks);
   104→  });
   105→
   106→  test('FALLBACK events show character name in the log entry', async ({ page }) => {
   107→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
   108→
   109→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
   110→    await waitForDashboard(page);
   111→
   112→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();
   113→
   114→    await page.waitForFunction(
   115→      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.fallback') !== null,
   116→      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }
   117→    );
   118→
   119→    const fallbackChars = await page.evaluate(() => {
   120→      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.fallback');
   121→      return Array.from(entries).map(e => e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim());
   122→    });
   123→
   124→    for (const charName of fallbackChars) {
   125→      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(charName);
   126→    }
   127→    console.log('Characters with FALLBACK events:', [...new Set(fallbackChars)]);
   128→  });
   129→
   130→  // ── RESERVATION RELEASE ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   131→
   132→  test('object slot is released when character plan changes (reservation cleared)', async ({ page }) => {
   133→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
   134→
   135→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
   136→    await waitForDashboard(page);
   137→
   138→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();
   139→
   140→    // Wait for a reserved slot to appear, then wait for it to clear
   141→    await page.waitForFunction(
   142→      () => document.querySelectorAll('.slot-pip.reserved').length > 0,
   143→      { timeout: 60_000, polling: 500 }
   144→    ).catch(() => null);
   145→
   146→    const initialReserved = await page.evaluate(() =>
   147→      document.querySelectorAll('.slot-pip.reserved').length
   148→    );
   149→
   150→    if (initialReserved === 0) {
   151→      console.log('[SKIP] No reserved slots observed — cannot verify release');
   152→      return;
   153→    }
   154→
   155→    console.log(`Initial reserved slots: ${initialReserved}`);
   156→
   157→    // Wait for a FALLBACK event (plan change should release the reservation)
   158→    await page.waitForFunction(
   159→      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.fallback') !== null,
   160→      { timeout: 60_000, polling: 500 }
   161→    ).catch(() => null);
   162→
   163→    // After fallback, give the UI a moment to update
   164→    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
   165→
   166→    // Verify that at some point after a fallback, reserved count decreases or goes to 0
   167→    // (it may go back up as characters make new plans, so we just verify it's not permanently stuck)
   168→    const afterFallback = await page.evaluate(() =>
   169→      document.querySelectorAll('.slot-pip.reserved').length
   170→    );
   171→
   172→    console.log(`Reserved slots after fallback: ${afterFallback}`);
   173→    // The slot count should have changed (either decreased or changed to occupied)
   174→    // This is a soft check — full slot release verification requires deterministic timing
   175→    // What we CAN assert: reserved slots count is a non-negative integer (no negative reservations)
   176→    expect(afterFallback).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
   177→  });
   178→
   179→  // ── FALLBACK + ALTERNATIVE SELECTION ─────────────────────────────────────
   180→
   181→  test('after FALLBACK, character transitions to a new action (not stuck)', async ({ page }) => {
   182→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
   183→
   184→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
   185→    await waitForDashboard(page);
   186→
   187→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();
   188→
   189→    // Wait for a FALLBACK event
   190→    const hasFallback = await page.waitForFunction(
   191→      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.fallback') !== null,
   192→      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }
   193→    ).catch(() => null);
   194→
   195→    if (!hasFallback) {
   196→      console.log('[SKIP] No FALLBACK observed — cannot verify post-fallback behavior');
   197→      return;
   198→    }
   199→
   200→    // Wait a moment for the character to react
   201→    await page.waitForTimeout(5000);
   202→
   203→    // All characters should still be in a valid action state (not frozen)
   204→    const charStates = await page.evaluate(() => {
   205→      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]');
   206→      return Array.from(cards).map(c => ({
   207→        id: c.id,
   208→        actionText: c.textContent.match(/Action\s+(\w+)/)?.[1] ?? 'unknown',
   209→      }));
   210→    });
   211→
   212→    expect(charStates.length).toBe(3);
   213→    for (const char of charStates) {
   214→      expect(
   215→        ['idle', 'walking', 'interacting', 'talking'],
   216→        `Character ${char.id} has unexpected action: ${char.actionText}`
   217→      ).toContain(char.actionText);
   218→    }
   219→    console.log('Character states after fallback:', charStates);
   220→  });
   221→});
   222→

    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-234\n     4\u2192 * Fallback Logic\n     5\u2192 *\n     6\u2192 * Verifies the fallback system described in the M1 spec:\n     7\u2192 *  - FALLBACK \"plan changed\" fires when AI updates a character's plan mid-walk\n     8\u2192 *  - FALLBACK \"unavailable\" fires when a character's target object is taken\n     9\u2192 *  - Characters pick an alternative object or go idle on fallback\n    10\u2192 *  - Object reservations are released when a character's plan changes\n    11\u2192 *\n    12\u2192 * Target: /debug.html (event log shows FALLBACK entries)\n    13\u2192 */\n    14\u2192const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n    17\u2192  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });\n    18\u2192  await page.waitForFunction(\n    19\u2192    () => {\n    20\u2192      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n    21\u2192      return cards.length >= 3 && cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');\n    22\u2192    },\n    23\u2192    { timeout }\n    24\u2192  );\n    25\u2192}\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192test.describe('M1: Fallback Logic', () => {\n    28\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 FALLBACK EVENTS IN EVENT LOG \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192  test('FALLBACK event appears in event log within 90 seconds', async ({ page }) => {\n    31\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192    await page.goto('/debug.html');\n    34\u2192    await waitForDashboard(page);\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192    // Enable 4x speed to see more AI cycles faster\n    37\u2192    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n    40\u2192      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.fallback') !== null,\n    41\u2192      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }\n    42\u2192    );\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192    const fallbackEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    45\u2192      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.fallback');\n    46\u2192      return Array.from(entries).map(e => ({\n    47\u2192        obj: e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim(),\n    48\u2192        transition: e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),\n    49\u2192        tag: e.querySelector('.log-tag')?.textContent.trim(),\n    50\u2192      }));\n    51\u2192    });\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192    expect(fallbackEntries.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    54\u2192    for (const entry of fallbackEntries) {\n    55\u2192      expect(entry.tag).toBe('FALLBACK');\n ▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {

    "id": "toolu_01F5FeQP7C4sKsotsn5UbiWM",
    "name": "Read",

USERMESSAGE #43
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * CRITICAL — FRG-184
     4→ * End-to-end sim loop integrity:
     5→ *   1. LLM decisions fire and are applied
     6→ *   2. Characters move (A*)
     7→ *   3. Object states change (visible in debug panel)
     8→ *   4. Sim clock advances in real-time
     9→ *   5. No console errors from simulation code
    10→ *
    11→ * Also documents the critical AI interact bug (FRG-184).
    12→ */
    13→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    14→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
    15→
    16→test.describe('Critical: Full sim loop integrity (FRG-184)', () => {
    17→  test('complete sim cycle: LLM fires → character moves → object interacted', async ({ page }) => {
    18→    const llmFired = [];
    19→    const simErrors = [];
    20→
    21→    page.on('request', req => {
    22→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
    23→        llmFired.push(req.url());
    24→      }
    25→    });
    26→
    27→    page.on('console', msg => {
    28→      if (msg.type() === 'error' && !msg.text().includes('favicon')) {
    29→        simErrors.push(msg.text());
    30→      }
    31→      // Catch AI engine warnings about bad responses
    32→      if (msg.type() === 'warn' && msg.text().includes('AIEngine')) {
    33→        simErrors.push(`[AIEngine WARN] ${msg.text()}`);
    34→      }
    35→    });
    36→
    37→    await page.goto('/');
    38→    await waitForEngine(page);
    39→
    40→    // Step 1: LLM decisions fire
    41→    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);
    42→    expect(llmFired.length, 'Expected at least 3 LLM requests').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    43→
    44→    // Step 2: Characters move
    45→    const walkState = await pollEngineState(
    46→      page,
    47→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    48→      15_000
    49→    );
    50→    expect(walkState.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking')).toBe(true);
    51→
    52→    // Step 3: An object changes state (from random wander which is always working)
    53→    const initial = await getEngineState(page);
    54→    const initialMap = Object.fromEntries(initial.objectStates.map(o => [o.id, o.state]));
    55→
    56→    const changedState = await pollEngineState(
    57→      page,
    58→      state => state.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]),
    59→      60_000,
    60→      500
    61→    );
    62→    const changed = changedState.objectStates.filter(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]);
    63→    expect(changed.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    64→
    65→    // Step 4: Sim clock advances
    66→    const t1 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    67→    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
    68→    const t2 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    69→    expect(t2).toBeGreaterThan(t1);
    70→
    71→    // Step 5: No sim errors
    72→    const criticalErrors = simErrors.filter(e =>
    73→      !e.includes('swiftshader') && !e.includes('WebGL') && !e.includes('ReadPixels')
    74→    );
    75→    if (criticalErrors.length > 0) {
    76→      console.warn('Simulation errors found:', criticalErrors);
    77→    }
    78→    expect(criticalErrors, 'Unexpected console errors in sim').toHaveLength(0);
    79→  });
    80→
    81→  test('[FIXED FRG-184] AI interact decisions no longer set _pendingInteract — targetObjectId pathway is used', async ({ page }) => {
    82→    /**
    83→     * Previously broken: AIEngine._applyDecision (interact case) set character._pendingInteract
    84→     * instead of character.targetObjectId, so SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival never fired.
    85→     *
    86→     * FIXED: _pendingInteract is no longer set by the AI engine. The correct property
    87→     * (targetObjectId) is now used, or the interaction is handled via a different pathway.
    88→     *
    89→     * This test verifies the OLD broken behavior is gone: after an AI "interact" decision,
    90→     * _pendingInteract must NOT be set on any character.
    91→     */
    92→    const interactDecisions = [];
    93→
    94→    page.on('response', async res => {
    95→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {
    96→        try {
    97→          const json = await res.json();
    98→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
    99→          if (text) {
   100→            const d = JSON.parse(text);
   101→            if (d.action === 'interact') interactDecisions.push({ ...d, ts: Date.now() });
   102→          }
   103→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   104→      }
   105→    });
   106→
   107→    await page.goto('/');
   108→    await waitForEngine(page);
   109→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
   110→
   111→    if (interactDecisions.length === 0) {
   112→      console.log('No AI "interact" decisions in this run — skipping bug verification');
   113→      return;
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    // After AI decides to interact, verify targetObjectId is NOT set
   117→    const charState = await page.evaluate(() =>
   118→      window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({
   119→        label: c.label,
   120→        pendingInteract: c._pendingInteract ?? null,
   121→        targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId ?? null,
   122→      }))
   123→    );
   124→
   125→    // Verify the fix: _pendingInteract must NOT be set by the AI engine
   126→    console.log('[FIXED FRG-184] Character state after AI interact decision:');
   127→    console.log(JSON.stringify(charState, null, 2));
   128→    console.log('AI interact decisions received:', interactDecisions);
   129→
   130→    for (const char of charState) {
   131→      expect(char.pendingInteract,
   132→        `[REGRESSION] ${char.label}: _pendingInteract is set — old broken code path is back`
   133→      ).toBeNull();
   134→    }
   135→  });
   136→
   137→  test('sim clock advances (simTimeMs increases over real time)', async ({ page }) => {
   138→    // Note: In headless Playwright, RAF is throttled (~25% of real time).
   139→    // We verify the clock advances but do not enforce the exact 60x speed ratio
   140→    // (that ratio holds in a real browser; see live browser validation above).
   141→    await page.goto('/');
   142→    await waitForEngine(page);
   143→
   144→    const t0 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
   145→    await page.waitForTimeout(5000); // 5 real seconds
   146→    const t1 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
   147→
   148→    const simElapsed = t1 - t0;
   149→
   150→    // Clock must advance (not frozen)
   151→    expect(simElapsed, 'simTimeMs did not advance — game loop may be broken').toBeGreaterThan(0);
   152→
   153→    // In headless, simSpeed=60 but RAF throttles to ~25% real time.
   154→    // Minimum acceptable: >5 sim seconds of advancement per 5 real seconds (1:1 floor).
   155→    expect(simElapsed, 'sim clock advancing too slowly').toBeGreaterThan(5000);
   156→
   157→    const ratio = simElapsed / 5000;
   158→    console.log(`Sim speed ratio: ${ratio.toFixed(1)}x (expected 60x in production, ${ratio.toFixed(1)}x in headless)`);
   159→  });
   160→
   161→  test('character mood and energy are initialised at 100', async ({ page }) => {
   162→    await page.goto('/');
   163→    await waitForEngine(page);
   164→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
   165→    for (const c of state.characters) {
   166→      expect(c.mood, `${c.label} mood`).toBe(100);
   167→      expect(c.energy, `${c.label} energy`).toBe(100);
   168→    }
   169→  });
   170→
   171→  test('no 5xx errors from /api/llm', async ({ page }) => {
   172→    const errors = [];
   173→    page.on('response', res => {
   174→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.status() >= 500) {
   175→        errors.push({ url: res.url(), status: res.status() });
   176→      }
   177→    });
   178→
   179→    await page.goto('/');
   180→    await waitForEngine(page);
   181→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);
   182→
   183→    expect(errors, 'Server errors from /api/llm').toHaveLength(0);
   184→  });
   185→});
   186→

    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * CRITICAL \u2014 FRG-184\n     4\u2192 * End-to-end sim loop integrity:\n     5\u2192 *   1. LLM decisions fire and are applied\n     6\u2192 *   2. Characters move (A*)\n     7\u2192 *   3. Object states change (visible in debug panel)\n     8\u2192 *   4. Sim clock advances in real-time\n     9\u2192 *   5. No console errors from simulation code\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Also documents the critical AI interact bug (FRG-184).\n    12\u2192 */\n    13\u2192const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n    14\u2192const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192test.describe('Critical: Full sim loop integrity (FRG-184)', () => {\n    17\u2192  test('complete sim cycle: LLM fires \u2192 character moves \u2192 object interacted', async ({ page }) => {\n    18\u2192    const llmFired = [];\n    19\u2192    const simErrors = [];\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192    page.on('request', req => {\n    22\u2192      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {\n    23\u2192        llmFired.push(req.url());\n    24\u2192      }\n    25\u2192    });\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192    page.on('console', msg => {\n    28\u2192      if (msg.type() === 'error' && !msg.text().includes('favicon')) {\n    29\u2192        simErrors.push(msg.text());\n    30\u2192      }\n    31\u2192      // Catch AI engine warnings about bad responses\n    32\u2192      if (msg.type() === 'warn' && msg.text().includes('AIEngine')) {\n    33\u2192        simErrors.push(`[AIEngine WARN] ${msg.text()}`);\n    34\u2192      }\n    35\u2192    });\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n    38\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192    // Step 1: LLM decisions fire\n    41\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);\n    42\u2192    expect(llmFired.length, 'Expected at least 3 LLM requests').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192    // Step 2: Characters move\n    45\u2192    const walkState = await pollEngineState(\n    46\u2192      page,\n    47\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n    48\u2192      15_000\n    49\u2192    );\n    50\u2192    expect(walkState.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking')).toBe(true);\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192    // Step 3: An object changes state (from random wander which is always working)\n    53\u2192    const initial = await getEngineState(page);\n    54\u2192    const initialMap = Object.fromEntries(initial.objectStates.map(o => [o.id, o.state]));\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192    const changedState = await pollEngineState(\n    57\u2192      page,\n    58\u2192      state => state.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]),\n    59\u2192      60_000,\n    60\u2192      500\n    61\u2192    );\n    62\u2192    const changed = changedState.objectStates.filter(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]);\n    63\u2192    expect(changed.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192    // Step 4: Sim clock advances\n    66\u2192    const t1 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);\n    67\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n    68\u2192    const t2 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);\n    69\u2192    expect(t2).toBeGreaterThan(t1);\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192    // Step 5: No sim errors\n    72\u2192    const criticalErrors = simErrors.filter(e =>\n    73\u2192      !e.includes('swiftshader') && !e.includes('WebGL') && !e.includes('ReadPixels')\n    74\u2192    );\n    75\u2192    if (criticalErrors.length > 0) {\n    76\u2192      console.warn('Simulation errors found:', criticalErrors);\n    77\u2192    }\n    78\u2192    expect(criticalErrors, 'Unexpected console errors in sim').toHaveLength(0);\n    79\u2192  });\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192  test('[FIXED FRG-184] AI interact decisions no longer set _pendingInteract \u2014 targetObjectId pathway is used', async ({ page }) => {\n    82\u2192    /**\n    83\u2192     * Previously broken: AIEngine._applyDecision (interact case) set character._pendingInteract\n    84\u2192     * instead of character.targetObjectId, so SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival never fired.\n    85\u2192     *\n    86\u2192     * FIXED: _pendingInteract is no longer set by the AI engine. The correct property\n    87\u2192     * (targetObjectId) is now used, or the interaction is handled via a different pathway.\n    88\u2192     *\n    89\u2192     * This test verifies the OLD broken behavior is gone: after an AI \"interact\" decision,\n    90\u2192     * _pendingInteract must NOT be set on any character.\n    91\u2192     */\n    92\u2192    const interactDecisions = [];\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192    page.on('response', async res => {\n    95\u2192      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {\n    96\u2192        try {\n    97\u2192          const json = await res.json();\n    98\u2192          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;\n    99\u2192          if (text) {\n   100\u2192            const d = JSON.parse(text);\n   101\u2192            if (d.action === 'interact') interactDecisions.push({ ...d, ts: Date.now() });\n   102\u2192          }\n   103\u2192        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n   104\u2192      }\n   105\u2192    });\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   108\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n   109\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192    if (interactDecisions.length === 0) {\n   112\u2192      console.log('No AI \"interact\" decisions in this run \u2014 skipping bug verification');\n   113\u2192      return;\n   114\u2192    }\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    // After AI decides to interact, verify targetObjectId is NOT set\n   117\u2192    const charState = await page.evaluate(() =>\n   118\u2192      window._gameEngine.characters.map(c => ({\n   119\u2192        label: c.label,\n   120\u2192        pendingInteract: c._pendingInteract ?? null,\n   121\u2192        targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId ?? null,\n   122\u2192      }))\n   123\u2192    );\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192    // Verify the fix: _pendingInteract must NOT be set by the AI engine\n   126\u2192    console.log('[FIXED FRG-184] Character state after AI interact decision:');\n   127\u2192    console.log(JSON.stringify(charState, null, 2));\n   128\u2192    console.log('AI interact decisions received:', interactDecisions);\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192    for (const char of charState) {\n   131\u2192      expect(char.pendingInteract,\n   132\u2192        `[REGRESSION] ${char.label}: _pendingInteract is set \u2014 old broken code path is back`\n   133\u2192      ).toBeNull();\n   134\u2192    }\n   135\u2192  });\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192  test('sim clock advances (simTimeMs increases over real time)', async ({ page }) => {\n   138\u2192    // Note: In headless Playwright, RAF is throttled (~25% of real time).\n   139\u2192    // We verify the clock advances but do not enforce the exact 60x speed ratio\n   140\u2192    // (that ratio holds in a real browser; see live browser validation above).\n   141\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   142\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192    const t0 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);\n   145\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(5000); // 5 real seconds\n   146\u2192    const t1 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192    const simElapsed = t1 - t0;\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192    // Clock must advance (not frozen)\n   151\u2192    expect(simElapsed, 'simTimeMs did not advance \u2014 game loop may be broken').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192    // In headless, simSpeed=60 but RAF throttles to ~25% real time.\n   154\u2192    // Minimum acceptable: >5 sim seconds of advancement per 5 real seconds (1:1 floor).\n   155\u2192    expect(simElapsed, 'sim clock advancing too slowly').toBeGreaterThan(5000);\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192    const ratio = simElapsed / 5000;\n   158\u2192    console.log(`Sim speed ratio: ${ratio.toFixed(1)}x (expected 60x in production, ${ratio.toFixed(1)}x in headless)`);\n   159\u2192  });\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192  test('character mood and energy are initialised at 100', async ({ page }) => {\n   162\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   163\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n   164\u2192    const state = await getEngineState(page);\n   165\u2192    for (const c of state.characters) {\n   166\u2192      expect(c.mood, `${c.label} mood`).toBe(100);\n   167\u2192      expect(c.energy, `${c.label} energy`).toBe(100);\n   168\u2192    }\n   169\u2192  });\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192  test('no 5xx errors from /api/llm', async ({ page }) => {\n   172\u2192    const errors = [];\n   173\u2192    page.on('response', res => {\n   174\u2192      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.status() >= 500) {\n   175\u2192        errors.push({ url: res.url(), status: res.status() });\n   176\u2192      }\n   177\u2192    });\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   180\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n   181\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(10000);\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192    expect(errors, 'Server errors from /api/llm').toHaveLength(0);\n   184\u2192  });\n   185\u2192});\n ASSISTANTMESSAGE #44
Now I have a comprehensive picture of the existing test suite. Let me analyze the gaps and areas for improvement by comparing against the Linear project description's feature spec: **Linear spec features:** 1. **Characters**: 3 characters: Maple, Sol, Nyx — ✅ Covered 2. **A* pathfinding** — ✅ Covered 3. **AI Decision Engine**: LLM every ~30s, 4 action types — ✅ Covered 4. **Smart Objects (Slot System)**: 10 objects, capacity, slot lifecycle — ✅ Covered 5. **Object State Machines**: idle→active→idle — ✅ Covered 6. **Talk Handshaking**: TALK/REJECT events, canTalk filter — ✅ Covered 7. **Fallback Logic**: FALLBACK events, plan changes, reservation release — ✅ Covered 8. **Debug Dashboard**: character cards, object cards, event log, speed controls, reset button — ✅ Covered 9. **Hold Timer**: timer visible in debug UI — ✅ Covered **Gaps I identified:** The reset button functionality is mentioned in the spec as clearing all state, repositioning characters, and resetting time, but it's only marked as a manual check in the test plan rather than having automated coverage. Speed controls for 2x and 4x are verified to exist and used to accelerate tests, but there's no actual test confirming the simulation clock advances at those rates when clicked. Multi-capacity objects like kotatsu and tv have tests checking pip counts and blocking behavior, but they don't verify that two characters can actually occupy the same object simultaneously. The LLM character targeting is covered through request body inspection in the talk-handshaking test, confirming nearby characters with canTalk status are included. Character facing direction during dialogue isn't tested—it's likely an engine-state observable that needs coverage. Similarly, the dialogue and description fields in LLM responses aren't validated; tests only check action and targetId/targetTile. Event log entries are tested for existence but lack deep format validation beyond what's covered in talk and fallback specs. The FALLBACK test checks for either "plan changed" or "unavailable" but doesn't isolate the "unavailable" path specifically. The AI request spec mentions characters should include facing direction, but I need to verify if that's actually being tested. There's also code duplication with `waitForDashboard` across four files that could be refactored into helpers, and a bug in the LLM response action validation test in m1-activity-duration.spec.js that needs fixing. Looking at the remaining test gaps: the reset button functionality isn't verified, and several character card fields—position, target, and facing—aren't being tested despite being in the spec. Multi-character capacity scenarios like two characters using the same object simultaneously are also missing coverage. For prioritization, I should focus on the infrastructure improvement of extracting the dashboard wait helper, then tackle the reset button test and speed control verification since those are more deterministic than the AI-dependent tests. The character card field tests and multi-capacity scenarios would follow, though some depend on non-deterministic sim behavior that's harder to pin down reliably. `getDebugState` helper for reading debug page state. Now I'm mapping out the specific test cases: reset functionality should clear the event log and restore the clock to morning, character cards need to display position coordinates and target information, speed controls should visibly affect simulation clock advancement, LLM responses must include dialogue or description fields, and the slot system tests should verify that multi-capacity items like kotatsu properly track simultaneous occupants. I'll update the imports across all spec files to use the new helpers.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #50
Now I have a complete picture. Let me assess the gaps: **From Linear spec vs current test coverage:** 1. **Reset button** — spec says "Reset button: clears all state, repositions characters, resets time." The dashboard test only checks it's visible. No automated test verifies the actual reset behavior. 2. **Speed controls (2x, 4x) functional** — spec says "1x, 2x, 4x". Tests verify existence. No test verifies clock actually advances faster. 3. **Character card: Position, Target, Facing** — spec says character cards show "name, position, action, target, facing, hold timer, AI countdown." Tests check action, mood, energy, hold timer, AI countdown. Missing from the cards: position (x,y), target, and facing direction. 4. **Multi-capacity simultaneous use** — spec says "two characters can use kotatsu/tv simultaneously." Tests verify pip counts and single-cap blocking, but not actual two-character simultaneous use of kotatsu/tv. 5. **LLM dialogue/description** — spec says "Decisions include dialogue text and description." The LLM response tests verify `action`, `targetId`, `targetTile` fields. No test checks for `dialogue` or `description` fields. 6. **Infrastructure: waitForDashboard duplication** — identical function is copied in `slot-system.spec.js`, `talk`... Now looking at test quality issues. The `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` test for LLM response action field has a timing problem — the response listener is set up in `beforeEach` via `waitForEngine` which already navigates, but then the test sets up another listener and waits for responses. There's some redundancy here in how the navigation and listener setup are structured. The real issue is that the response listener needs to be registered before navigation happens, not after. The `ai-decisions` tests handle this correctly by setting up network listeners in their beforeEach before any navigation occurs. I need to fix the flaky test pattern by moving the listener setup earlier in the flow, and I should also add a shared `waitForDashboard` helper to reduce duplication across the test files. Then I'll update the regression tests to cover the reset button behavior, character card fields like Position and Target, and verify that the 2x speed control actually accelerates the clock. Most impactful fixes are the infrastructure refactor for `waitForDashboard`, the reset button coverage gap, character card fields, LLM dialogue field validation, and speed control functional test. The multi-capacity simultaneous use test is harder to make reliable given the non-deterministic AI behavior. For the flaky `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` test, the issue is that `beforeEach` navigates before the response listener is attached, so the initial LLM requests fire before we can capture them—I need to either navigate fresh within the test itself or wait for the next 30-second cycle. The test setup navigates and waits for the engine to initialize, then the test itself registers a listener and waits 10 seconds. The issue is that some initial LLM responses might fire before the listener is registered, so they'd be missed. But the test might still pass if those responses happen slowly enough or within the 10-second window after the listener starts. The test might actually be working because the LLM API latency is longer than the test setup time, so responses arrive after the listener is registered—it's fragile but functional in practice. Now I'm shifting focus to concrete improvements: I need to add a `waitForDashboard` helper function, then write several new tests covering dashboard functionality like the reset button, character card fields, and speed multiplier behavior, plus extend the AI decisions test suite. Add test verifying LLM responses include `dialogue` (or `description`) field 4. **slot-system.spec.js**: - Import `waitForDashboard` from helpers (remove local copy) - Add test: kotatsu and tv can show 2 slots occupied simultaneously 5. **talk-handshaking.spec.js**: Import `waitForDashboard` from helpers (remove local copy) 6. **fallback-logic.spec.js**: Import `waitForDashboard` from helpers (remove local copy) 7. Update README to reflect new tests The character cards display a countdown timer for the next action. Looking at the spec, they should also show position, target, and facing direction, but the existing dashboard test doesn't verify these fields. I should write tests that check for them based on what the spec describes. For the reset button, I can verify it works by checking that the clock resets to the simulation start time, the event log clears, and character stats return to their initial values. For speed control, I'll test clicking through the different speed options and sampling the behavior. The character card's target field should display the object ID or name when a character is walking toward something, and facing is likely a direction string like "left" or "right". Now I'm planning the test strategy: I'll extract the dashboard wait helper to reduce duplication, then write focused tests for the missing gaps in the spec. Since the dashboard should display name, position, action, target, facing, hold timer, and AI countdown according to the spec, I'll verify these fields are actually visible in the character cards. For the reset button, I'll test that clicking it resets the clock, and for speed controls, I'll confirm the simulation advances faster at higher speeds. Now I need to set up the helpers file... Extracting the dashboard wait function that checks for the grid, event log, and character cards with action text... Now I'm thinking through the test cases: for the LLM dialogue test, I need to verify that the JSON response includes the action and target fields, plus any dialogue or description data. For multi-capacity scenarios, I'll make the test graceful since simultaneous character placement is probabilistic. I also need to check that character cards display position information in some form, show their current target when walking, and indicate which direction they're facing. But I'm realizing I don't actually know the exact HTML structure of these dashboard fields. Looking at how the existing tests check for Action, Mood, and Energy labels using text matching, I could do the same for Position, Target, and Facing. The tricky part is that these fields might not be implemented yet in the dashboard—the spec describes what should be there, but the actual implementation might be incomplete. Since the task requires all tests to pass in CI, I need to be strategic about what I test. I could write tests that check for these fields if they exist, but make them flexible enough to handle slight variations in naming or structure. Or I could start with a more conservative approach and only test what I can verify is actually present in the DOM. For the Reset button specifically, I'm thinking the best verification would be to check if the simulation clock resets to an early morning time like 8:00 or 8:01 AM. If the sim runs at 60x speed, after 20 seconds of real time the clock would advance about 20 minutes, so I could click reset and verify the time jumps back to the start rather than continuing forward. For speed controls, testing the exact ratio between 1x and 2x might be unreliable due to RAF throttling in headless environments. A more robust approach would be to switch to 4x speed, wait several seconds, and verify the clock advanced significantly more than it would have at 1x speed over the same duration. I'm settling on four concrete test priorities: extracting the waitForDashboard helper for reusability, testing the reset button's functionality, verifying speed controls work at different multipliers, and checking that LLM responses populate the dialogue and description fields correctly. For the character card position display, I'll write a flexible test that looks for any position-like indicator on the card without being strict about the exact format—whether it's coordinates, a label, or some other representation. The key is focusing on tests that fill real coverage gaps, are likely to pass against the current deployment, and meaningfully improve quality without introducing fragile assertions. I'll also need to refactor a few existing test files afterward. I'm thinking through the implementation strategy: I'll add a `waitForDashboard` helper to the test infrastructure, then organize the work into commits covering the dashboard test additions, LLM dialogue field tests, multi-capacity slot system tests, and documentation updates. Starting with the helpers and refactoring existing specs to use the new utility. Now I need to consolidate the `waitForDashboard` function across the test files—it's currently defined in multiple places like `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js`, `slot-system.spec.js`, and `talk-handshaking.spec.js`, so I'll move it to a shared helpers module and update all the specs to import from there. Writing the reset test... Finishing the time parsing logic... I'm working through the test logic: if the sim starts at 8:00 AM and runs for 8 real seconds at 60x speed, that's 8 minutes of sim time, putting the clock at 8:08 AM. After a reset, it should jump back to an earlier time like 8:00 AM, so the comparison `minAfter < minBefore` should hold. But I need to verify what the actual starting sim time is in the test setup, since the smoke test only checks that the time format is valid without specifying the initial value. If it's running at 60x real-time, then 5 seconds would show 5 minutes of sim time at 1x and 10 minutes at 2x, but the debug page might use a completely different timing mechanism that doesn't match that assumption. -time by default (no Phaser speed multiplier), with the 2x/4x buttons being multipliers on top of that. So at 1x in debug, 1 real second = 1 sim second. At 2x, 1 real second = 2 sim seconds. But these aren't minutes... the clock shows "8:05 AM" which is sim TIME in hours:minutes, but what's the conversion? If sim time is set up like: - 1 real second = 1 minute of sim time (at 1x) - At 2x: 1 real second = 2 minutes of sim time Then in 5 real seconds at 1x → 5 sim minutes → delta = 5 In 5 real seconds at 2x → 10 sim minutes → delta = 10 This seems plausible and testable. The debug page might run at a similar speed to the Phaser page with its `simSpeed = 60` setting. Looking at the test that checks if the clock advances over 5 real seconds, it just verifies the time changed without measuring the exact amount—which makes sense since even at 1x speed, 5 seconds would shift the display noticeably. But since the clock displays hours and minutes, I need to figure out how many sim seconds actually translate to a visible minute change on the display. At 2x speed, the clock advances 10 minutes in 5 seconds, and at 4x it's 20 minutes—so comparing the deltas should reliably detect the speed multiplier. But there's a wraparound problem when the sim clock crosses from AM to PM, which would break my minute parsing logic. Actually, crossing noon works fine since both times stay in the same day format. The real issue is midnight—if the clock goes from 11:55 PM to 12:05 AM, I'd get a negative delta that needs adjustment. For a short test window though, I can probably skip handling that edge case. I'll use the more thorough version across all specs. I'll combine both versions for consistency in the helpers, using the dashboard approach that waits for both the event log and character cards. Now I'm writing a test to verify that character cards display position coordinates in the expected format, waiting a few seconds for the characters to settle into their positions before checking the text content. Hmm, this is too uncertain. Let me look at what regex I can use. Given the spec says \"(x, y)\" style, maybe it's just the numbers in parentheses like \"(3, 5)\". Or the card labels it as \"Pos\" followed by numbers.\n\nActually, let me look at what's already tested. The m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js test at line 151 says `\"/debug.html` shows position (x, y) in character card\" (from TEST_PLAN_M1.md line 127). And the test 7.4 says \"Character cards show Action, Mood, Energy\" but not Position. So there's a TEST PLAN item for position but no test for it in the spec. The question is whether the DOM actually has position info.\n\nI'll write a test checking for the position field and see. Since this test goes against a live deployment, if the field exists the test passes.\n\nFor the Reset test, I need to be careful about timing. After clicking Reset, the sim resets to starting time, the event log should clear or show a reset entry, and character states reset. The key observable is checking the clock display for an early time. I'll write a test that waits for the sim to advance a bit, reads the clock time, clicks reset, then checks that the clock shows an earlier time or fresh start time. The tricky part is that sim time is measured in sim-time like \"8:15 AM\" rather than real time, so I need to account for how fast the simulation runs.\n\nActually, I don't know what speed the debug dashboard runs at by default. Rather than trying to measure exact times, I'll verify that the clock shows a time before the pre-reset time as a regression test, or check that the event log has fewer entries after reset, or simply verify that `simTimeMs` is less after reset than before.\n\nFor the speed control test, I'm comparing `simTimeMs` advancement between 1x and 2x modes.\n\nWriting speed comparison test... I'm realizing the debug page uses a different engine architecture than the Phaser page\u2014it doesn't expose `window._sim` or `window._gameEngine`, instead relying on DOM elements like `#clock` and `#event-log` to track state. I need to find the right internal reference or DOM property to measure simulation time on the debug page.\n\nActually, I can parse the clock display directly\u2014converting \"8:05 AM\" to minutes since midnight and comparing deltas across real-time waits to verify the speed multiplier is working. Let me write the test logic for this approach.\n\nI'm thinking through the implementation strategy: I'll add a `waitForDashboard` helper to the test infrastructure, then organize the work into commits covering the dashboard test additions, LLM dialogue field tests, multi-capacity slot system tests, and documentation updates. Starting with the helpers and refactoring existing specs to use the new utility.\n\nNow I need to consolidate the `waitForDashboard` function across the test files\u2014it's currently defined in multiple places like `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js`, `slot-system.spec.js`, and `talk-handshaking.spec.js`, so I'll move it to a shared helpers module and update all the specs to import from there.\n\nFor the new reset button test, I'm writing a test that lets the simulation run for a few seconds to advance the clock, then verifies the reset functionality works correctly using only DOM selectors since the debug page doesn't expose internal state through the window object.\n\nWriting the reset test... Finishing the time parsing logic...\n\nI'm working through the test logic: if the sim starts at 8:00 AM and runs for 8 real seconds at 60x speed, that's 8 minutes of sim time, putting the clock at 8:08 AM. After a reset, it should jump back to an earlier time like 8:00 AM, so the comparison `minAfter < minBefore` should hold. But I need to verify what the actual starting sim time is in the test setup, since the smoke test only checks that the time format is valid without specifying the initial value.\n\nI'll make the test robust by waiting 10 seconds for the sim to advance noticeably, then triggering a reset and checking that the clock time decreases\u2014this way it doesn't matter what the exact starting time or debug speed is, as long as there's measurable forward progress before the reset.\n\nWriting the test function... Writing test assertions... Writing the speed comparison test...\n\nActually, I'm realizing there's a problem with my approach\u2014the clock only shows minute-level precision with AM/PM formatting, so over just 5 seconds I might not see enough change to reliably measure the speed difference.\n\nLet me reconsider: the debug page likely runs the simulation at a different speed than the main game, and I don't actually know what that speed is. If it's running at 60x real-time, then 5 seconds would show 5 minutes of sim time at 1x and 10 minutes at 2x, but the debug page might use a completely different timing mechanism that doesn't match that assumption. -time by default (no Phaser speed multiplier), with the 2x/4x buttons being multipliers on top of that. So at 1x in debug, 1 real second = 1 sim second. At 2x, 1 real second = 2 sim seconds. But these aren't minutes... the clock shows \"8:05 AM\" which is sim TIME in hours:minutes, but what's the conversion?\n\nIf sim time is set up like:\n- 1 real second = 1 minute of sim time (at 1x)\n- At 2x: 1 real second = 2 minutes of sim time\n\nThen in 5 real seconds at 1x \u2192 5 sim minutes \u2192 delta = 5\nIn 5 real seconds at 2x \u2192 10 sim minutes \u2192 delta = 10\n\nThis seems plausible and testable. The debug page might run at a similar speed to the Phaser page with its `simSpeed = 60` setting. Looking at the test that checks if the clock advances over 5 real seconds, it just verifies the time changed without measuring the exact amount\u2014which makes sense since even at 1x speed, 5 seconds would shift the display noticeably. But since the clock displays hours and minutes, I need to figure out how many sim seconds actually translate to a visible minute change on the display. At 2x speed, the clock advances 10 minutes in 5 seconds, and at 4x it's 20 minutes\u2014so comparing the deltas should reliably detect the speed multiplier. But there's a wraparound problem when the sim clock crosses from AM to PM, which would break my minute parsing logic.\n\nActually, crossing noon works fine since both times stay in the same day format. The real issue is midnight\u2014if the clock goes from 11:55 PM to 12:05 AM, I'd get a negative delta that needs adjustment. For a short test window though, I can probably skip handling that edge case.\n\nNow I'm thinking about the character card structure and what fields I need to track from the Linear spec.\n\nWriting test code... I'm going to work through the implementation systematically\u2014updating the helpers, adding the new tests to the dashboard spec, then moving through the other spec files to use the shared helper and add the missing tests. For the character card facing test, I need to check what the facing direction actually looks like in the debug output, since characters always have a facing value set regardless of whether they're idle, walking, or interacting.\n\nWriting test code... Writing test for dialogue and description fields... Writing test assertions... Writing the test logic... Now I'm finishing the assertion and logging the results, then shifting focus to refactoring the `waitForDashboard` helper\u2014I need to extract it into a shared helpers file and update the imports across multiple test files, being careful to preserve the exact test count breakdown mentioned in the README. I'm looking at the `waitForDashboard` function and realizing I need to standardize it across all spec files\u2014the version in `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` has some differences, so I'll extract the most complete version to helpers.js and make sure the imports are consistent everywhere. Now I need to actually implement these changes and double-check the import statement when I add it to that file.\n\nThere's also a question about whether the LLM response actually includes `dialogue` and `description` fields as the spec claims\u2014the existing tests only check for `action`, `targetId`, and `targetTile`. I should write the test to assert that these fields exist, since the spec says they should be there, and if they're missing that's a real product issue worth catching. Now I'm laying out the concrete changes I need to make across the test files: extracting the shared `waitForDashboard` helper into a common module, adding specific test cases for the dashboard features like position display and facing direction, verifying the LLM returns dialogue or description fields, and testing multi-capacity slot occupancy.\n\nFor the character card position test, I need to figure out the exact format the dashboard uses\u2014whether it displays as \"Pos: (3, 5)\" or some other pattern\u2014so I can write a reliable assertion that matches the actual output. I'm ready to start implementing these test changes now.",

My analysis of gaps from the Linear spec: 1. **waitForDashboard** duplicated in 4 files → extract to helpers 2. **Character card: Position field** — spec says cards show position (x, y). Not tested. 3. **Character card: Facing field** — spec says cards show facing. Not tested. 4. **Reset button behavior** — spec says "clears all state, repositions characters, resets time." Only visibility tested, not behavior. 5. **Speed control functional test** — spec says 2x/4x exist. Visibility tested, not behavior. 6. **LLM response: dialogue + description** — spec says "Decisions include dialogue text and description." Not tested. 7. **Multi-capacity simultaneous use** — spec says "two characters can use kotatsu/tv simultaneously." Not tested. Now I'll write all the changes.

Character card: **Position** (x, y) \u2014 spec lists it, not tested\n3. Character card: **Facing** direction \u2014 spec lists it, not tested  \n4. **Reset button behavior** \u2014 only visibility tested, not actual reset\n5. **Speed controls functional** \u2014 visibility only, not clock speed verification\n6. **LLM response `dialogue`/`description` fields** \u2014 spec says included, not tested\n7. **Multi-capacity simultaneous occupancy** \u2014 spec says kotatsu/tv support 2 users, not tested"

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #55
Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/helpers.js",
  "old_string": "module.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState };",
  "new_string": "/**\n * Wait for /debug.html dashboard to finish loading.\n * Shared by m1-debug-dashboard, slot-system, talk-handshaking, fallback-logic specs.\n * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n * @param {number} [timeout=20000]\n */\nasync function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });\n  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => {\n      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n      if (cards.length < 3) return false;\n      return cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');\n    },\n    { timeout }\n  );\n}\n\nmodule.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard };"
}

      "replace_all": false,
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/helpers.js",
      "old_string": "module.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState };",
      "new_string": "/**\n * Wait for /debug.html dashboard to finish loading.\n * Shared by m1-debug-dashboard, slot-system, talk-handshaking, fallback-logic specs.\n * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n * @param {number} [timeout=20000]\n */\nasync function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });\n  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => {\n      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n      if (cards.length < 3) return false;\n      return cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');\n    },\n    { timeout }\n  );\n}\n\nmodule.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard };"
    },

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #65
Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js",
  "old_string": "const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n\n/** Wait for the debug dashboard to finish loading (objects + characters grid populated). */\nasync function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });\n  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });\n  // Wait until at least one character card renders an action value\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => {\n      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n      if (cards.length < 3) return false;\n      // Check that character cards have content\n      return cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');\n    },\n    { timeout }\n  );\n}",
  "new_string": "const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForDashboard } = require('../helpers');\n\n/** Parse a sim clock string (\"8:05 AM\") to total sim-minutes since midnight. */\nfunction parseSimMinutes(text) {\n  const m = text.match(/(\\d+):(\\d{2})\\s*(AM|PM)/i);\n  if (!m) return 0;\n  let h = parseInt(m[1]);\n  const min = parseInt(m[2]);\n  const period = m[3].toUpperCase();\n  if (period === 'PM' && h !== 12) h += 12;\n  if (period === 'AM' && h === 12) h = 0;\n  return h * 60 + min;\n}"
}

{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",

      "replace_all": false,
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js",
      "old_string": "const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n\n/** Wait for the debug dashboard to finish loading (objects + characters grid populated). */\nasync function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });\n  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });\n  // Wait until at least one character card renders an action value\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => {\n      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n      if (cards.length < 3) return false;\n      // Check that character cards have content\n      return cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');\n    },\n    { timeout }\n  );\n}",
      "new_string": "const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForDashboard } = require('../helpers');\n\n/** Parse a sim clock string (\"8:05 AM\") to total sim-minutes since midnight. */\nfunction parseSimMinutes(text) {\n  const m = text.match(/(\\d+):(\\d{2})\\s*(AM|PM)/i);\n  if (!m) return 0;\n  let h = parseInt(m[1]);\n  const min = parseInt(m[2]);\n  const period = m[3].toUpperCase();\n  if (period === 'PM' && h !== 12) h += 12;\n  if (period === 'AM' && h === 12) h = 0;\n  return h * 60 + min;\n}"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

  "old_string": "const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n\n/** Wait for the debug dashboard to finish loading (objects + characters grid populated). */\nasync function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });\n  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });\n  // Wait until at least one character card renders an action value\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => {\n      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n      if (cards.length < 3) return false;\n      // Check that character cards have content\n      return cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');\n    },\n    { timeout }\n  );\n}",
  "new_string": "const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForDashboard } = require('../helpers');\n\n/** Parse a sim clock string (\"8:05 AM\") to total sim-minutes since midnight. */\nfunction parseSimMinutes(text) {\n  const m = text.match(/(\\d+):(\\d{2})\\s*(AM|PM)/i);\n  if (!m) return 0;\n  let h = parseInt(m[1]);\n  const min = parseInt(m[2]);\n  const period = m[3].toUpperCase();\n  if (period === 'PM' && h !== 12) h += 12;\n  if (period === 'AM' && h === 12) h = 0;\n  return h * 60 + min;\n}"
}

    "input": {
      "replace_all": false,
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js",
      "old_string": "const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n\n/** Wait for the debug dashboard to finish loading (objects + characters grid populated). */\nasync function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });\n  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });\n  // Wait until at least one character card renders an action value\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    () => {\n      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n      if (cards.length < 3) return false;\n      // Check that character cards have content\n      return cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');\n    },\n    { timeout }\n  );\n}",
      "new_string": "const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\nconst { waitForDashboard } = require('../helpers');\n\n/** Parse a sim clock string (\"8:05 AM\") to total sim-minutes since midnight. */\nfunction parseSimMinutes(text) {\n  const m = text.match(/(\\d+):(\\d{2})\\s*(AM|PM)/i);\n  if (!m) return 0;\n  let h = parseInt(m[1]);\n  const min = parseInt(m[2]);\n  const period = m[3].toUpperCase();\n  if (period === 'PM' && h !== 12) h += 12;\n  if (period === 'AM' && h === 12) h = 0;\n  return h * 60 + min;\n}"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js",
  "old_string": "  test('Pause button stops the sim clock', async ({ page }) => {\n    const time1 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    await page.locator('#btn-pause').click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(3000);\n    const time2 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    expect(time2, 'Clock should be frozen when paused').toBe(time1);\n    // Resume\n    await page.locator('#btn-pause').click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n    const time3 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    expect(time3, 'Clock should advance after resume').not.toBe(time2);\n  });\n});",
  "new_string": "  test('Pause button stops the sim clock', async ({ page }) => {\n    const time1 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    await page.locator('#btn-pause').click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(3000);\n    const time2 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    expect(time2, 'Clock should be frozen when paused').toBe(time1);\n    // Resume\n    await page.locator('#btn-pause').click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n    const time3 = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    expect(time3, 'Clock should advance after resume').not.toBe(time2);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 CHARACTER CARD FIELDS \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('character cards show tile position (x, y)', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Wait a moment for character positions to render\n    await page.waitForTimeout(3000);\n    const card0Text = await page.locator('#char-0').innerText();\n    // Spec: character cards display \"position\" \u2014 look for \"Pos\" label or coordinate pattern\n    expect(card0Text, 'Character card should display position').toMatch(/Pos|Position|\\(\\d+,\\s*\\d+\\)/i);\n  });\n\n  test('character cards show Facing direction', async ({ page }) => {\n    await page.waitForTimeout(3000);\n    const card0Text = await page.locator('#char-0').innerText();\n    // Spec: character cards display \"facing\" direction\n    expect(card0Text, 'Character card should display facing direction').toMatch(/Facing|facing/i);\n  });\n\n  test('character card shows Target field when walking toward an object', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Wait for a character to start walking (they walk toward a target object/tile)\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => {\n        const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n        return Array.from(cards).some(c => c.textContent.toLowerCase().includes('walking'));\n      },\n      { timeout: 20_000 }\n    );\n\n    // At least one walking character card should show a Target field\n    const walkingCardTexts = await page.evaluate(() =>\n      Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]'))\n        .filter(c => c.textContent.toLowerCase().includes('walking'))\n        .map(c => c.textContent.trim())\n    );\n\n    for (const text of walkingCardTexts) {\n      expect(text, 'Walking character card should display Target field').toMatch(/Target|target/i);\n    }\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 RESET BUTTON \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('Reset button clears event log and restores sim to initial state', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Wait for event log to populate with entries\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => {\n        const log = document.getElementById('event-log');\n        return log && log.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length > 0;\n      },\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const countBefore = await page.evaluate(\n      () => document.getElementById('event-log')?.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length ?? 0\n    );\n    expect(countBefore, 'Event log should have entries before reset').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n\n    // Click Reset\n    await page.locator('#btn-reset').click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n\n    // Event log should clear (or significantly reduce)\n    const countAfter = await page.evaluate(\n      () => document.getElementById('event-log')?.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length ?? 0\n    );\n    expect(countAfter, 'Event log should clear after reset').toBeLessThan(countBefore);\n\n    // Clock should show an AM time (sim restarts from morning)\n    const clockText = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    expect(clockText).toMatch(/\\d+:\\d{2} (AM|PM)/);\n    expect(clockText, `Clock should show AM time after reset (got ${clockText})`).toContain('AM');\n\n    // Sim should continue running after reset (clock advances)\n    const minBefore = parseSimMinutes(clockText);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(4000);\n    const clockAfter = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    const minAfter = parseSimMinutes(clockAfter);\n    expect(minAfter, 'Clock should advance after reset').toBeGreaterThan(minBefore);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 SPEED CONTROLS \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('2x speed advances sim clock faster than 1x speed', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Ensure 1x is active, measure clock advancement over 5s\n    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '1x' }).click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n\n    const start1x = parseSimMinutes(await page.locator('#clock').innerText());\n    await page.waitForTimeout(5000);\n    const end1x = parseSimMinutes(await page.locator('#clock').innerText());\n    const delta1x = end1x - start1x;\n\n    // Switch to 2x, measure advancement over same 5s window\n    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '2x' }).click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n\n    const start2x = parseSimMinutes(await page.locator('#clock').innerText());\n    await page.waitForTimeout(5000);\n    const end2x = parseSimMinutes(await page.locator('#clock').innerText());\n    const delta2x = end2x - start2x;\n\n    console.log(`1x: +${delta1x} sim-min in 5s | 2x: +${delta2x} sim-min in 5s`);\n\n    // 2x should advance at least 1.4x faster than 1x (tolerates timing variance)\n    expect(delta2x, `2x speed (${delta2x} sim-min) should advance faster than 1x (${delta1x} sim-min)`).toBeGreaterThan(delta1x * 1.4);\n\n    // Reset to 1x for subsequent tests\n    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '1x' }).click();\n  });\n});"
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #78
Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/ai-decisions.spec.js",
  "old_string": "  test('/api/llm endpoint returns HTTP 200 with valid JSON', async ({ page }) => {\n    const statuses = [];\n\n    page.on('response', res => {\n      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {\n        statuses.push(res.status());\n      }\n    });\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);\n\n    expect(statuses.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n    for (const status of statuses) {\n      expect(status).toBe(200);\n    }\n  });\n});",
  "new_string": "  test('/api/llm endpoint returns HTTP 200 with valid JSON', async ({ page }) => {\n    const statuses = [];\n\n    page.on('response', res => {\n      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {\n        statuses.push(res.status());\n      }\n    });\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);\n\n    expect(statuses.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n    for (const status of statuses) {\n      expect(status).toBe(200);\n    }\n  });\n\n  test('LLM responses include dialogue text and description fields', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Spec: \"Decisions include dialogue text and description\"\n    // These fields drive speech bubbles and the event log narrative.\n    const responses = [];\n\n    page.on('response', async res => {\n      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {\n        try {\n          const json = await res.json();\n          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;\n          if (text) responses.push(JSON.parse(text));\n        } catch { /* ignore malformed */ }\n      }\n    });\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);\n\n    expect(responses.length, 'Should have captured at least 3 LLM decisions').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n\n    // Every response must have a string dialogue field (drives speech bubbles)\n    // and a string description field (drives event log narrative)\n    for (const decision of responses) {\n      expect(decision, 'LLM decision should include dialogue field').toHaveProperty('dialogue');\n      expect(typeof decision.dialogue, `dialogue should be a string, got ${typeof decision.dialogue}`).toBe('string');\n      expect(decision.dialogue.length, 'dialogue should not be empty').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n\n      expect(decision, 'LLM decision should include description field').toHaveProperty('description');\n      expect(typeof decision.description, `description should be a string, got ${typeof decision.description}`).toBe('string');\n      expect(decision.description.length, 'description should not be empty').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    }\n\n    const actionBreakdown = responses.reduce((acc, r) => {\n      acc[r.action] = (acc[r.action] || 0) + 1;\n      return acc;\n    }, {});\n    console.log('LLM responses \u2014 action breakdown:', actionBreakdown);\n    console.log('Sample dialogue:', responses[0]?.dialogue);\n    console.log('Sample description:', responses[0]?.description);\n  });\n});"
}

      "new_string": "  test('/api/llm endpoint returns HTTP 200 with valid JSON', async ({ page }) => {\n    const statuses = [];\n\n    page.on('response', res => {\n      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {\n        statuses.push(res.status());\n      }\n    });\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(8000);\n\n    expect(statuses.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n    for (const status of statuses) {\n      expect(status).toBe(200);\n    }\n  });\n\n  test('LLM responses include dialogue text and description fields', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Spec: \"Decisions include dialogue text and description\"\n    // These fields drive speech bubbles and the event log narrative.\n    const responses = [];\n\n    page.on('response', async res => {\n      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {\n        try {\n          const json = await res.json();\n          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;\n          if (text) responses.push(JSON.parse(text));\n        } catch { /* ignore malformed */ }\n      }\n    });\n\n    await page.goto('/');\n    await waitForEngine(page);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);\n\n    expect(responses.length, 'Should have captured at least 3 LLM decisions').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);\n\n    // Every response must have a string dialogue field (drives speech bubbles)\n    // and a string description field (drives event log narrative)\n    for (const decision of responses) {\n      expect(decision, 'LLM decision should include dialogue field').toHaveProperty('dialogue');\n      expect(typeof decision.dialogue, `dialogue should be a string, got ${typeof decision.dialogue}`).toBe('string');\n      expect(decision.dialogue.length, 'dialogue should not be empty').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n\n      expect(decision, 'LLM decision should include description field').toHaveProperty('description');\n      expect(typeof decision.description, `description should be a string, got ${typeof decision.description}`).toBe('string');\n      expect(decision.description.length, 'description should not be empty').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    }\n\n    const actionBreakdown = responses.reduce((acc, r) => {\n      acc[r.action] = (acc[r.action] || 0) + 1;\n      return acc;\n    }, {});\n    console.log('LLM responses \u2014 action breakdown:', actionBreakdown);\n    console.log('Sample dialogue:', responses[0]?.dialogue);\n    console.log('Sample description:', responses[0]?.description);\n  });\n});"
    },

      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
