ASSISTANTMESSAGE #83
Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js",
  "old_string": "  test('single-capacity object pip becomes reserved when character heads there', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n\n    // Single-capacity object IDs\n    const singleCapIds = ['tea_set', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge', 'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel'];\n\n    // Wait for any single-capacity object to be reserved\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      (ids) => ids.some(id => {\n        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;\n      }),\n      singleCapIds,\n      { timeout: 45_000, polling: 500 }\n    );\n\n    const reservedSingle = await page.evaluate((ids) =>\n      ids.filter(id => {\n        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;\n      }),\n      singleCapIds\n    );\n\n    expect(reservedSingle.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    console.log('Single-cap objects with reserved slot:', reservedSingle);\n  });\n});",
  "new_string": "  test('single-capacity object pip becomes reserved when character heads there', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n\n    // Single-capacity object IDs\n    const singleCapIds = ['tea_set', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge', 'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel'];\n\n    // Wait for any single-capacity object to be reserved\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      (ids) => ids.some(id => {\n        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;\n      }),\n      singleCapIds,\n      { timeout: 45_000, polling: 500 }\n    );\n\n    const reservedSingle = await page.evaluate((ids) =>\n      ids.filter(id => {\n        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;\n      }),\n      singleCapIds\n    );\n\n    expect(reservedSingle.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    console.log('Single-cap objects with reserved slot:', reservedSingle);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 MULTI-CAPACITY SIMULTANEOUS USE \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('multi-capacity object (kotatsu or tv) shows both slots occupied simultaneously', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Spec: \"two characters can use kotatsu/tv simultaneously\"\n    // This is probabilistic \u2014 the AI must direct two characters to the same object.\n    // We observe and verify; gracefully skip if not observed in this run.\n    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n\n    const multiCapIds = ['kotatsu', 'tv'];\n\n    const foundMultiOccupied = await page.waitForFunction(\n      (ids) => {\n        for (const id of ids) {\n          const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n          if (!el) continue;\n          const pips = el.querySelectorAll('.slot-pip');\n          // Both pips non-free (reserved or occupied)\n          const nonFree = Array.from(pips).filter(p => !p.classList.contains('free'));\n          if (nonFree.length >= 2) return true;\n          // Or the label explicitly shows \"full\" (all slots taken)\n          const label = el.querySelector('.slot-label');\n          if (label && label.textContent.trim() === 'full') return true;\n        }\n        return false;\n      },\n      multiCapIds,\n      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }\n    ).catch(() => null);\n\n    if (!foundMultiOccupied) {\n      console.log('[INFO] Multi-capacity simultaneous occupation not observed in this run. ' +\n        'AI must direct two characters to the same kotatsu or tv \u2014 non-deterministic.');\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Verify the multi-cap object state\n    const state = await page.evaluate((ids) =>\n      ids.map(id => {\n        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n        if (!el) return { id, error: 'element not found' };\n        const pips = el.querySelectorAll('.slot-pip');\n        const label = el.querySelector('.slot-label');\n        return {\n          id,\n          totalPips: pips.length,\n          nonFreePips: Array.from(pips).filter(p => !p.classList.contains('free')).length,\n          label: label?.textContent.trim() ?? null,\n        };\n      }),\n      multiCapIds\n    );\n\n    const fullyOccupied = state.filter(o => o.nonFreePips >= 2 || o.label === 'full');\n    expect(fullyOccupied.length, 'At least one multi-cap object should show both slots taken').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    // Multi-cap objects should have 2 pips\n    for (const obj of fullyOccupied) {\n      expect(obj.totalPips, `${obj.id} should have 2 pips (capacity=2)`).toBe(2);\n    }\n    console.log('Multi-capacity objects with both slots occupied:', fullyOccupied);\n  });\n});"
}

      "old_string": "  test('single-capacity object pip becomes reserved when character heads there', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n\n    // Single-capacity object IDs\n    const singleCapIds = ['tea_set', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge', 'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel'];\n\n    // Wait for any single-capacity object to be reserved\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      (ids) => ids.some(id => {\n        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;\n      }),\n      singleCapIds,\n      { timeout: 45_000, polling: 500 }\n    );\n\n    const reservedSingle = await page.evaluate((ids) =>\n      ids.filter(id => {\n        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;\n      }),\n      singleCapIds\n    );\n\n    expect(reservedSingle.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    console.log('Single-cap objects with reserved slot:', reservedSingle);\n  });\n});",
      "new_string": "  test('single-capacity object pip becomes reserved when character heads there', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(60_000);\n\n    // Single-capacity object IDs\n    const singleCapIds = ['tea_set', 'stove', 'bonsai', 'fridge', 'bed', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel'];\n\n    // Wait for any single-capacity object to be reserved\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      (ids) => ids.some(id => {\n        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;\n      }),\n      singleCapIds,\n      { timeout: 45_000, polling: 500 }\n    );\n\n    const reservedSingle = await page.evaluate((ids) =>\n      ids.filter(id => {\n        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n        return el && el.querySelector('.slot-pip.reserved') !== null;\n      }),\n      singleCapIds\n    );\n\n    expect(reservedSingle.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    console.log('Single-cap objects with reserved slot:', reservedSingle);\n  });\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 MULTI-CAPACITY SIMULTANEOUS USE \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n  test('multi-capacity object (kotatsu or tv) shows both slots occupied simultaneously', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Spec: \"two characters can use kotatsu/tv simultaneously\"\n    // This is probabilistic \u2014 the AI must direct two characters to the same object.\n    // We observe and verify; gracefully skip if not observed in this run.\n    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n\n    const multiCapIds = ['kotatsu', 'tv'];\n\n    const foundMultiOccupied = await page.waitForFunction(\n      (ids) => {\n        for (const id of ids) {\n          const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n          if (!el) continue;\n          const pips = el.querySelectorAll('.slot-pip');\n          // Both pips non-free (reserved or occupied)\n          const nonFree = Array.from(pips).filter(p => !p.classList.contains('free'));\n          if (nonFree.length >= 2) return true;\n          // Or the label explicitly shows \"full\" (all slots taken)\n          const label = el.querySelector('.slot-label');\n          if (label && label.textContent.trim() === 'full') return true;\n        }\n        return false;\n      },\n      multiCapIds,\n      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }\n    ).catch(() => null);\n\n    if (!foundMultiOccupied) {\n      console.log('[INFO] Multi-capacity simultaneous occupation not observed in this run. ' +\n        'AI must direct two characters to the same kotatsu or tv \u2014 non-deterministic.');\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Verify the multi-cap object state\n    const state = await page.evaluate((ids) =>\n      ids.map(id => {\n        const el = document.getElementById('slots-' + id);\n        if (!el) return { id, error: 'element not found' };\n        const pips = el.querySelectorAll('.slot-pip');\n        const label = el.querySelector('.slot-label');\n        return {\n          id,\n          totalPips: pips.length,\n          nonFreePips: Array.from(pips).filter(p => !p.classList.contains('free')).length,\n          label: label?.textContent.trim() ?? null,\n        };\n      }),\n      multiCapIds\n    );\n\n    const fullyOccupied = state.filter(o => o.nonFreePips >= 2 || o.label === 'full');\n    expect(fullyOccupied.length, 'At least one multi-cap object should show both slots taken').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    // Multi-cap objects should have 2 pips\n    for (const obj of fullyOccupied) {\n      expect(obj.totalPips, `${obj.id} should have 2 pips (capacity=2)`).toBe(2);\n    }\n    console.log('Multi-capacity objects with both slots occupied:', fullyOccupied);\n  });\n});"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

USERMESSAGE #89
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * Shared helpers for AI Aquarium QA tests.
     4→ * All tests run against the deployed BASE_URL via window._gameEngine.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→/**
     8→ * Wait for Phaser to finish booting and the SimulationEngine to be attached.
     9→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    10→ * @param {number} [timeout=20000]
    11→ */
    12→async function waitForEngine(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    13→  await page.waitForFunction(
    14→    () => window._gameEngine &&
    15→          window._gameEngine.characters &&
    16→          window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,
    17→    { timeout }
    18→  );
    19→}
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * Read the live engine state.
    23→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    24→ */
    25→async function getEngineState(page) {
    26→  return page.evaluate(() => {
    27→    const eng = window._gameEngine;
    28→    return {
    29→      simTime: eng.getSimTimeString(),
    30→      aiEnabled: eng.aiEngine?.enabled ?? false,
    31→      characters: eng.characters.map(c => ({
    32→        name: c.name,
    33→        label: c.label,
    34→        tx: c.tx,
    35→        ty: c.ty,
    36→        action: c.action,
    37→        mood: c.mood,
    38→        energy: c.energy,
    39→      })),
    40→      objectStates: eng.objectEngine.getAll().map(o => ({
    41→        id: o.id,
    42→        label: o.label,
    43→        state: o.state,
    44→      })),
    45→    };
    46→  });
    47→}
    48→
    49→/**
    50→ * Poll engine state until predicate returns truthy, or timeout.
    51→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    52→ * @param {(state: object) => boolean} predicate
    53→ * @param {number} [timeoutMs=30000]
    54→ * @param {number} [intervalMs=500]
    55→ */
    56→async function pollEngineState(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 30_000, intervalMs = 500) {
    57→  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
    58→  while (Date.now() < deadline) {
    59→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    60→    if (predicate(state)) return state;
    61→    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);
    62→  }
    63→  throw new Error(`pollEngineState timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);
    64→}
    65→
    66→/**
    67→ * Wait for /debug.html dashboard to finish loading.
    68→ * Shared by m1-debug-dashboard, slot-system, talk-handshaking, fallback-logic specs.
    69→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    70→ * @param {number} [timeout=20000]
    71→ */
    72→async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    73→  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });
    74→  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });
    75→  await page.waitForFunction(
    76→    () => {
    77→      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]');
    78→      if (cards.length < 3) return false;
    79→      return cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');
    80→    },
    81→    { timeout }
    82→  );
    83→}
    84→
    85→module.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard };
    86→

    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * Shared helpers for AI Aquarium QA tests.\n     4\u2192 * All tests run against the deployed BASE_URL via window._gameEngine.\n     5\u2192 */\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192/**\n     8\u2192 * Wait for Phaser to finish booting and the SimulationEngine to be attached.\n     9\u2192 * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n    10\u2192 * @param {number} [timeout=20000]\n    11\u2192 */\n    12\u2192async function waitForEngine(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n    13\u2192  await page.waitForFunction(\n    14\u2192    () => window._gameEngine &&\n    15\u2192          window._gameEngine.characters &&\n    16\u2192          window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,\n    17\u2192    { timeout }\n    18\u2192  );\n    19\u2192}\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192/**\n    22\u2192 * Read the live engine state.\n    23\u2192 * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n    24\u2192 */\n    25\u2192async function getEngineState(page) {\n    26\u2192  return page.evaluate(() => {\n    27\u2192    const eng = window._gameEngine;\n    28\u2192    return {\n    29\u2192      simTime: eng.getSimTimeString(),\n    30\u2192      aiEnabled: eng.aiEngine?.enabled ?? false,\n    31\u2192      characters: eng.characters.map(c => ({\n    32\u2192        name: c.name,\n    33\u2192        label: c.label,\n    34\u2192        tx: c.tx,\n    35\u2192        ty: c.ty,\n    36\u2192        action: c.action,\n    37\u2192        mood: c.mood,\n    38\u2192        energy: c.energy,\n    39\u2192      })),\n    40\u2192      objectStates: eng.objectEngine.getAll().map(o => ({\n    41\u2192        id: o.id,\n    42\u2192        label: o.label,\n    43\u2192        state: o.state,\n    44\u2192      })),\n    45\u2192    };\n    46\u2192  });\n    47\u2192}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192/**\n    50\u2192 * Poll engine state until predicate returns truthy, or timeout.\n    51\u2192 * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n    52\u2192 * @param {(state: object) => boolean} predicate\n    53\u2192 * @param {number} [timeoutMs=30000]\n    54\u2192 * @param {number} [intervalMs=500]\n    55\u2192 */\n    56\u2192async function pollEngineState(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 30_000, intervalMs = 500) {\n    57\u2192  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\n    58\u2192  while (Date.now() < deadline) {\n    59\u2192    const state = await getEngineState(page);\n    60\u2192    if (predicate(state)) return state;\n    61\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);\n    62\u2192  }\n    63\u2192  throw new Error(`pollEngineState timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192/**\n    67\u2192 * Wait for /debug.html dashboard to finish loading.\n    68\u2192 * Shared by m1-debug-dashboard, slot-system, talk-handshaking, fallback-logic specs.\n    69\u2192 * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n    70\u2192 * @param {number} [timeout=20000]\n    71\u2192 */\n    72\u2192async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n    73\u2192  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });\n    74\u2192  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });\n    75\u2192  await page.waitForFunction(\n    76\u2192    () => {\n    77\u2192      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^=\"char-\"]');\n    78\u2192      if (cards.length < 3) return false;\n    79\u2192      return cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');\n    80\u2192    },\n    81\u2192    { timeout }\n    82\u2192  );\n    83\u2192}\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192module.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard };\n    86\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.   }

     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-209
     4→ * M1 Test Plan: Debug Dashboard (/debug.html)
     5→ *
     6→ * Tests the full debug dashboard which is the M1 foundation.
     7→ * The debug page runs the sim engine headlessly and provides real-time visibility
     8→ * into: character states, object states, and the event log.
     9→ *
    10→ * Target: /debug.html (not the main Phaser page — that is M2).
    11→ */
    12→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    13→const { waitForDashboard } = require('../helpers');
    14→
    15→/** Parse a sim clock string ("8:05 AM") to total sim-minutes since midnight. */
    16→function parseSimMinutes(text) {
    17→  const m = text.match(/(\d+):(\d{2})\s*(AM|PM)/i);
    18→  if (!m) return 0;
    19→  let h = parseInt(m[1]);
    20→  const min = parseInt(m[2]);
    21→  const period = m[3].toUpperCase();
    22→  if (period === 'PM' && h !== 12) h += 12;
    23→  if (period === 'AM' && h === 12) h = 0;
    24→  return h * 60 + min;
    25→}
    26→
    27→test.describe('M1: Debug Dashboard — /debug.html', () => {
    28→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    29→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
    30→    await waitForDashboard(page);

    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-209\n     4\u2192 * M1 Test Plan: Debug Dashboard (/debug.html)\n     5\u2192 *\n     6\u2192 * Tests the full debug dashboard which is the M1 foundation.\n     7\u2192 * The debug page runs the sim engine headlessly and provides real-time visibility\n     8\u2192 * into: character states, object states, and the event log.\n     9\u2192 *\n    10\u2192 * Target: /debug.html (not the main Phaser page \u2014 that is M2).\n    11\u2192 */\n    12\u2192const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n    13\u2192const { waitForDashboard } = require('../helpers');\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192/** Parse a sim clock string (\"8:05 AM\") to total sim-minutes since midnight. */\n    16\u2192function parseSimMinutes(text) {\n    17\u2192  const m = text.match(/(\\d+):(\\d{2})\\s*(AM|PM)/i);\n    18\u2192  if (!m) return 0;\n    19\u2192  let h = parseInt(m[1]);\n    20\u2192  const min = parseInt(m[2]);\n    21\u2192  const period = m[3].toUpperCase();\n    22\u2192  if (period === 'PM' && h !== 12) h += 12;\n    23\u2192  if (period === 'AM' && h === 12) h = 0;\n    24\u2192  return h * 60 + min;\n    25\u2192}\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192test.describe('M1: Debug Dashboard \u2014 /debug.html', () => {\n    28\u2192  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n    29\u2192    await page.goto('/debug.html');\n    30\u2192    await waitForDashboard(page);\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. ASSISTANTMESSAGE #101
Good progress! We have 86 passed, 1 failed, 1 flaky. Let me analyze the failures: 1. **Reset button test fails**: The reset doesn't fully clear the event log - after reset, 2 entries remain (probably reset/restart events themselves). The test expects `countAfter < countBefore` and countBefore=1, countAfter=2. This means the reset ADDS entries (reset event log entries) rather than clearing to 0. I need to rethink this test. The behavior: before reset, there was 1 entry. After reset, there are 2. So the reset is adding its own event log entries. I think the issue is that I need to wait for more entries to accumulate before clicking reset so there's a meaningful difference to measure, or I should change the assertion to verify the clock resets instead of checking the log count. Adjusting the assertion to match the new wait condition — with at least 3 entries before reset and only 1-2 appearing immediately after, the count should clearly decrease. I'm also noting that the interactTimerMax test is a pre-existing flaky issue unrelated to my changes, so I'll focus on fixing the reset test first.

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js",
  "old_string": "  test('Reset button clears event log and restores sim to initial state', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Wait for event log to populate with entries\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => {\n        const log = document.getElementById('event-log');\n        return log && log.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length > 0;\n      },\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const countBefore = await page.evaluate(\n      () => document.getElementById('event-log')?.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length ?? 0\n    );\n    expect(countBefore, 'Event log should have entries before reset').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n\n    // Click Reset\n    await page.locator('#btn-reset').click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n\n    // Event log should clear (or significantly reduce)\n    const countAfter = await page.evaluate(\n      () => document.getElementById('event-log')?.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length ?? 0\n    );\n    expect(countAfter, 'Event log should clear after reset').toBeLessThan(countBefore);\n\n    // Clock should show an AM time (sim restarts from morning)\n    const clockText = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    expect(clockText).toMatch(/\\d+:\\d{2} (AM|PM)/);\n    expect(clockText, `Clock should show AM time after reset (got ${clockText})`).toContain('AM');\n\n    // Sim should continue running after reset (clock advances)\n    const minBefore = parseSimMinutes(clockText);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(4000);\n    const clockAfter = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    const minAfter = parseSimMinutes(clockAfter);\n    expect(minAfter, 'Clock should advance after reset').toBeGreaterThan(minBefore);\n  });",
  "new_string": "  test('Reset button clears event log and restores sim to initial state', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n\n    // Wait for event log to accumulate enough entries to clearly measure before/after.\n    // The sim fires AI decisions quickly, so we expect \u22655 entries within 30s.\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => {\n        const log = document.getElementById('event-log');\n        return log && log.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length >= 5;\n      },\n      { timeout: 30_000 }\n    );\n\n    const countBefore = await page.evaluate(\n      () => document.getElementById('event-log')?.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length ?? 0\n    );\n    expect(countBefore, 'Event log should have \u22655 entries before reset').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5);\n\n    // Also record the sim clock time before reset\n    const clockBefore = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    const minsBefore = parseSimMinutes(clockBefore);\n\n    // Click Reset\n    await page.locator('#btn-reset').click();\n    // Give the UI a moment to apply the reset and fire any immediate new events\n    await page.waitForTimeout(1000);\n\n    // Event log should have significantly fewer entries than before reset.\n    // The new sim may fire 1-2 initial events immediately, so allow a small buffer.\n    const countAfter = await page.evaluate(\n      () => document.getElementById('event-log')?.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length ?? 0\n    );\n    expect(\n      countAfter,\n      `Event log after reset (${countAfter}) should be much less than before (${countBefore})`\n    ).toBeLessThan(Math.ceil(countBefore / 2));\n\n    // Clock should have reset to an earlier time (start of the sim day)\n    const clockAfter = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    expect(clockAfter).toMatch(/\\d+:\\d{2} (AM|PM)/);\n    const minsAfter = parseSimMinutes(clockAfter);\n    expect(\n      minsAfter,\n      `Clock (${clockAfter}) should show an earlier time than before reset (${clockBefore})`\n    ).toBeLessThan(minsBefore);\n\n    // Sim should continue running after reset (clock advances again)\n    await page.waitForTimeout(4000);\n    const clockAdvanced = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    expect(clockAdvanced, 'Clock should advance after reset').not.toBe(clockAfter);\n  });"
}

      "old_string": "  test('Reset button clears event log and restores sim to initial state', async ({ page }) => {\n    // Wait for event log to populate with entries\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => {\n        const log = document.getElementById('event-log');\n        return log && log.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length > 0;\n      },\n      { timeout: 15_000 }\n    );\n\n    const countBefore = await page.evaluate(\n      () => document.getElementById('event-log')?.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length ?? 0\n    );\n    expect(countBefore, 'Event log should have entries before reset').toBeGreaterThan(0);\n\n    // Click Reset\n    await page.locator('#btn-reset').click();\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n\n    // Event log should clear (or significantly reduce)\n    const countAfter = await page.evaluate(\n      () => document.getElementById('event-log')?.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length ?? 0\n    );\n    expect(countAfter, 'Event log should clear after reset').toBeLessThan(countBefore);\n\n    // Clock should show an AM time (sim restarts from morning)\n    const clockText = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    expect(clockText).toMatch(/\\d+:\\d{2} (AM|PM)/);\n    expect(clockText, `Clock should show AM time after reset (got ${clockText})`).toContain('AM');\n\n    // Sim should continue running after reset (clock advances)\n    const minBefore = parseSimMinutes(clockText);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(4000);\n    const clockAfter = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    const minAfter = parseSimMinutes(clockAfter);\n    expect(minAfter, 'Clock should advance after reset').toBeGreaterThan(minBefore);\n  });",
      "new_string": "  test('Reset button clears event log and restores sim to initial state', async ({ page }) => {\n    test.setTimeout(90_000);\n\n    // Wait for event log to accumulate enough entries to clearly measure before/after.\n    // The sim fires AI decisions quickly, so we expect \u22655 entries within 30s.\n    await page.waitForFunction(\n      () => {\n        const log = document.getElementById('event-log');\n        return log && log.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length >= 5;\n      },\n      { timeout: 30_000 }\n    );\n\n    const countBefore = await page.evaluate(\n      () => document.getElementById('event-log')?.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length ?? 0\n    );\n    expect(countBefore, 'Event log should have \u22655 entries before reset').toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5);\n\n    // Also record the sim clock time before reset\n    const clockBefore = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    const minsBefore = parseSimMinutes(clockBefore);\n\n    // Click Reset\n    await page.locator('#btn-reset').click();\n    // Give the UI a moment to apply the reset and fire any immediate new events\n    await page.waitForTimeout(1000);\n\n    // Event log should have significantly fewer entries than before reset.\n    // The new sim may fire 1-2 initial events immediately, so allow a small buffer.\n    const countAfter = await page.evaluate(\n      () => document.getElementById('event-log')?.querySelectorAll('.log-entry').length ?? 0\n    );\n    expect(\n      countAfter,\n      `Event log after reset (${countAfter}) should be much less than before (${countBefore})`\n    ).toBeLessThan(Math.ceil(countBefore / 2));\n\n    // Clock should have reset to an earlier time (start of the sim day)\n    const clockAfter = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    expect(clockAfter).toMatch(/\\d+:\\d{2} (AM|PM)/);\n    const minsAfter = parseSimMinutes(clockAfter);\n    expect(\n      minsAfter,\n      `Clock (${clockAfter}) should show an earlier time than before reset (${clockBefore})`\n    ).toBeLessThan(minsBefore);\n\n    // Sim should continue running after reset (clock advances again)\n    await page.waitForTimeout(4000);\n    const clockAdvanced = await page.locator('#clock').innerText();\n    expect(clockAdvanced, 'Clock should advance after reset').not.toBe(clockAfter);\n  });"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #105
Now let me also fix the `interactTimerMax` test. Looking at the failure: it tries to poll for an interacting character within 20s. The issue is that with the beforeEach navigation already done and the test starting, the characters might not have started interacting yet within 20s. Looking at the test: ```js test('interactTimerMax values fall within expected object duration ranges', async ({ page }) => { test.setTimeout(60_000); // Wait for at least one character to interact await pollEngineState( page, state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'), 20_000 ); ``` The 20-second timeout might be too tight since characters could be between activities when the test runs. I think bumping that up to 30 seconds should give enough buffer for at least one character to enter an interacting state.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "thinking",
    "thinking": "Now let me also fix the `interactTimerMax` test. Looking at the failure: it tries to poll for an interacting character within 20s. The issue is that with the beforeEach navigation already done and the test starting, the characters might not have started interacting yet within 20s.\n\nLooking at the test:\n```js\ntest('interactTimerMax values fall within expected object duration ranges', async ({ page }) => {\n  test.setTimeout(60_000);\n\n  // Wait for at least one character to interact\n  await pollEngineState(\n    page,\n    state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'interacting'),\n    20_000\n  );\n```\n\nThe 20-second timeout might be too tight since characters could be between activities when the test runs. I think bumping that up to 30 seconds should give enough buffer for at least one character to enter an interacting state.",
    "signature": "EsUKCkYICxgCKkB5d62MSC4lGoW6f/1lsBxXYpEU/kqyJowOngh8HbvX5dGrWeqQbernOzHolG80E7XqNlwBom+IRkRVlYoH1DkWEgwMowljQFcSV6UzXuoaDO89f8pI71Wj7TLQXyIwBRlu9rzC6FBPRtipcu9fFhhBgBisg+d0jiWUmDc2HuRvvvyDoO0uuf0qjLui+MbHKqwJz3aWeW+J6S3LlvgJBqv07pkXjLsl5gRu38xPCJElV86lNEyM5dyFfekExtxwzydSw5H5GhMZL/ufTzTb5WhlsJi4O2Nu9IT0u3DOQQGIlrCAZjaxiQMXomNXUeGFtglQ9XobzHJoWNr31PJwdLIot0F4bKBbcZaaYq7fY4IjQ1rTPE/etjX+B/5PXcp1ThVvKKkQAbPw5gOh6rzVar/4LJKOgXbQ3JS5YJ360c+xfIcgkz2gBl0GpZj/ONwq8X0p++6fAG7qp9lwWvKIJDRV9VM8dgMy9e08UlAYYQIsc+1/lE/3lKM9dHgGepPC416kxTP/zNZHD6ENiupAGLLnuYP0i0EJ0UIpoI0ze860XsYACVD3ky+fJo7ePPc+Ht3hTnI1TBQQhqbpwxIOYf1hEpXpFrfkGJ6j5CE1l6c079h1LQ3MAc8L5KzreEEC33IeiSYPOMJGxixMGxmJyI/3R55j2yYlbWD8MCQJ1H2jS9A3Azq5mQQy+thzhOzWFVCeKSK3W8knyoaj5bFyVDZHDPOeAD6fry4bKJE87+sFhnWKXQgtD9qQ03jghus7aR+MG2/9OjKGjNm0W7k1Qm3dhy7+PYHZWAiw6x0DikO4ppj7t+yd8DfWQHYV7cj26xJIlK+fMrSix5DMnNfxWo7LZpFNFUc9IxjAjTYoVY6EVOoDkgIbfPLYtzNh2HBt3sDDzIaXSit9xs087eZEgJ/9RBEaXmiKWV8+akMC+CA7OySLARF2FSsgfZjConkZ1pcL2p6JUqw0Kq7TKn5V0/Y3blNReJkiNMX2vCSp+WK/C3cFcvrquKcvIBZrph5t1SjmNWGJhz9UNI9TQdHEPVdfNl/tWs9Nx8OeqAiz26cupzLQHeYv9+/BQeWBxjC2Lk7XufnD/rM0NklK9QBaXklN9EHSCyXQhTVwzGEuBhwaK1BNYL4PV1dPRzDfhjIaYa81VmAXw2I1eFewYwI/x4x4rAedjHyrNlfzlOWTQsBWcFfnzdSwhPe/3C0wMZJCS1oAptIT9LHTLRyCYPWV4kW5rVM6tPB16uzxSVt90PjCzqWKezuV2Yh3hRajHxFa8kEtPziDDXCaFFpaNWa60U5sTpPuKJmoHic5qrOehw3GEpn1gjL5jwpEuFt3uGfB9VIZt3U3Y803wYcJ5oa3F65o+cuMgTLU3hR+GHgn0d3a3ByRz1yIqAWq67t7NspuwzNyckaP8hV42hhw5itb8+ad7EbpAzP0rNYdS8n1v3+Pmeo5GtbnSoWqVm3pTWmd0pDSm4OkqKos7erwXSltA/Fg+6WCbbtfFvcePgZDuRdmoutRxYLSWwTF9gN31bUiWZDX/oIK+XJFQ44wG/m5GBWjNPHhhoFtvcfIwB8NKzjDgOYbjabtrtMdKvlATDfsGYt5tHgJBUyGROJEydJqzyStH4PBWIM5cyuD9hjgdZyLrL9dPZDeDLNK6cXs6eP6n6TmGgoaOZTm+o/NcoZaN3jEBEJ6GvYKcRR5hOeRm4KJWvocoYX9MgrEp9FWI/MZRnsbGpyr8bbBiEIwlCqo5ekLoyOSmMzwSSCgfxWtTUdHkdNfbMyyE66GulI/K3UYAQ=="

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/README.md",
  "old_string": "## Test Structure\n\n```\ntests/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 helpers.js                          # Shared helpers (waitForEngine, pollEngineState)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 smoke/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 app-loads.spec.js               # Page loads, engine init, 3 characters, 10 objects\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 regression/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 ai-decisions.spec.js            # AI LLM requests, valid action types, 30s interval\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 character-movement.spec.js      # A* pathfinding, tile movement, bounds check\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 object-interaction.spec.js      # Object state transitions, debug panel\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 speech-bubbles.spec.js          # Dialogue lines, pendingSpeech, speech timer\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js      # Debug page layout, real-time updates, pause/resume\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 m1-activity-duration.spec.js    # Activity timers, 30s AI cycle, action types\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 slot-system.spec.js             # Slot/capacity display, pip indicators, reservations\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 talk-handshaking.spec.js        # TALK/REJECT events, talking state, canTalk filter\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 fallback-logic.spec.js          # FALLBACK events, plan changes, reservation release\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 critical/\n    \u2514\u2500\u2500 sim-loop-integrity.spec.js      # End-to-end: LLM \u2192 move \u2192 object change \u2192 no errors\n```",
  "new_string": "## Test Structure\n\n```\ntests/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 helpers.js                          # Shared helpers (waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 smoke/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 app-loads.spec.js               # Page loads, engine init, 3 characters, 10 objects\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 regression/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 ai-decisions.spec.js            # AI LLM requests, valid action types, 30s interval, dialogue fields\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 character-movement.spec.js      # A* pathfinding, tile movement, bounds check\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 object-interaction.spec.js      # Object state transitions, debug panel\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 speech-bubbles.spec.js          # Dialogue lines, pendingSpeech, speech timer\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js      # Debug page layout, real-time updates, pause/resume,\n\u2502   \u2502                                   #   position/facing/target fields, reset behavior, speed controls\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 m1-activity-duration.spec.js    # Activity timers, 30s AI cycle, action types\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 slot-system.spec.js             # Slot/capacity display, pip indicators, reservations,\n\u2502   \u2502                                   #   multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu/tv)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 talk-handshaking.spec.js        # TALK/REJECT events, talking state, canTalk filter\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 fallback-logic.spec.js          # FALLBACK events, plan changes, reservation release\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 critical/\n    \u2514\u2500\u2500 sim-loop-integrity.spec.js      # End-to-end: LLM \u2192 move \u2192 object change \u2192 no errors\n```"
}

Adding those up: 8 + 6 + 4 + 5 + 4 + 24 + 10 + 11 + 6 + 5 + 5 = 88. ✓ I need to update the README to reflect 24 tests for m1-debug-dashboard instead of 25, and change the total from 89 to 88. ASSISTANTMESSAGE #148
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat(qa): enhance M1 regression suite \u2014 fill spec gaps, refactor infrastructure\n\nGap analysis vs Linear project spec identified 7 missing coverage areas.\nAll 88 tests pass.\n\nInfrastructure:\n- Extract waitForDashboard() to helpers.js (was duplicated in 4 spec files)\n- Import shared helper in slot-system, talk-handshaking, fallback-logic, m1-debug-dashboard\n\nNew tests (7 added, 81 \u2192 88):\n- ai-decisions: LLM responses include dialogue + description fields (spec: \"decisions include dialogue text and description\")\n- m1-debug-dashboard: character card shows tile position (x, y)\n- m1-debug-dashboard: character card shows Facing direction\n- m1-debug-dashboard: character card shows Target field when walking\n- m1-debug-dashboard: Reset button clears event log and restores sim to morning time\n- m1-debug-dashboard: 2x speed advances sim clock faster than 1x (functional, not just visibility)\n- slot-system: multi-capacity object (kotatsu/tv) shows both slots occupied simultaneously\n\nBug fixes:\n- m1-activity-duration: extend interactTimerMax poll from 20s \u2192 30s (pre-existing flaky timeout)\n- m1-debug-dashboard: reset test waits for \u22655 log entries before asserting clear (was racing with initial event burst)\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\"",
  "description": "Commit all QA improvements"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01MCs6ZF5qtQDpNLjL4F2tsG",
    "name": "Bash",
    "input": {
      "command": "git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat(qa): enhance M1 regression suite \u2014 fill spec gaps, refactor infrastructure\n\nGap analysis vs Linear project spec identified 7 missing coverage areas.\nAll 88 tests pass.\n\nInfrastructure:\n- Extract waitForDashboard() to helpers.js (was duplicated in 4 spec files)\n- Import shared helper in slot-system, talk-handshaking, fallback-logic, m1-debug-dashboard\n\nNew tests (7 added, 81 \u2192 88):\n- ai-decisions: LLM responses include dialogue + description fields (spec: \"decisions include dialogue text and description\")\n- m1-debug-dashboard: character card shows tile position (x, y)\n- m1-debug-dashboard: character card shows Facing direction\n- m1-debug-dashboard: character card shows Target field when walking\n- m1-debug-dashboard: Reset button clears event log and restores sim to morning time\n- m1-debug-dashboard: 2x speed advances sim clock faster than 1x (functional, not just visibility)\n- slot-system: multi-capacity object (kotatsu/tv) shows both slots occupied simultaneously\n\nBug fixes:\n- m1-activity-duration: extend interactTimerMax poll from 20s \u2192 30s (pre-existing flaky timeout)\n- m1-debug-dashboard: reset test waits for \u22655 log entries before asserting clear (was racing with initial event burst)\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\"",
      "description": "Commit all QA improvements"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #157
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/.claude/projects/-home-gem-project/memory/MEMORY.md",
  "content": "# AI Aquarium QA Repo Memory\n\n## Project\n- QA repo for https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app\n- Tests: Playwright, chromium headless, tests against live deployment\n- Repo: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa\n- Linear project: 0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63\n\n## Current State (2026-03-08)\n- 88 tests, 88/88 passing\n- Push directly to main (no PRs)\n\n## Key Architecture\n- `tests/helpers.js`: waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard (all shared)\n- Tests against `/` (Phaser page, window._gameEngine) and `/debug.html` (dashboard)\n- Non-deterministic AI behavior handled via graceful skip patterns (return early, console.log)\n- `waitForDashboard()` shared helper: waits for #characters-grid, #event-log, char cards with \"Action\"\n\n## Test Structure (88 tests)\n- smoke/app-loads: 8\n- regression/ai-decisions: 6 (incl. dialogue/description field test)\n- regression/character-movement: 4\n- regression/object-interaction: 5\n- regression/speech-bubbles: 4\n- regression/m1-debug-dashboard: 24 (incl. position, facing, target, reset, speed)\n- regression/m1-activity-duration: 10\n- regression/slot-system: 11 (incl. multi-cap simultaneous use)\n- regression/talk-handshaking: 6\n- regression/fallback-logic: 5\n- critical/sim-loop-integrity: 5\n\n## Key Conventions\n- Slow/probabilistic tests use test.setTimeout(120_000) or 180_000\n- Non-deterministic events (REJECT, talk, multi-cap) use graceful skip: if (!found) { console.log(...); return; }\n- Network interceptors (page.on('request'/'response')) must be set up BEFORE page.goto()\n- pollEngineState polls window._gameEngine; debug page tests use DOM selectors only\n- `parseSimMinutes(text)` helper in m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js for clock comparisons\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

      "file_path": "/home/gem/.claude/projects/-home-gem-project/memory/MEMORY.md",
      "content": "# AI Aquarium QA Repo Memory\n\n## Project\n- QA repo for https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app\n- Tests: Playwright, chromium headless, tests against live deployment\n- Repo: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa\n- Linear project: 0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63\n\n## Current State (2026-03-08)\n- 88 tests, 88/88 passing\n- Push directly to main (no PRs)\n\n## Key Architecture\n- `tests/helpers.js`: waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard (all shared)\n- Tests against `/` (Phaser page, window._gameEngine) and `/debug.html` (dashboard)\n- Non-deterministic AI behavior handled via graceful skip patterns (return early, console.log)\n- `waitForDashboard()` shared helper: waits for #characters-grid, #event-log, char cards with \"Action\"\n\n## Test Structure (88 tests)\n- smoke/app-loads: 8\n- regression/ai-decisions: 6 (incl. dialogue/description field test)\n- regression/character-movement: 4\n- regression/object-interaction: 5\n- regression/speech-bubbles: 4\n- regression/m1-debug-dashboard: 24 (incl. position, facing, target, reset, speed)\n- regression/m1-activity-duration: 10\n- regression/slot-system: 11 (incl. multi-cap simultaneous use)\n- regression/talk-handshaking: 6\n- regression/fallback-logic: 5\n- critical/sim-loop-integrity: 5\n\n## Key Conventions\n- Slow/probabilistic tests use test.setTimeout(120_000) or 180_000\n- Non-deterministic events (REJECT, talk, multi-cap) use graceful skip: if (!found) { console.log(...); return; }\n- Network interceptors (page.on('request'/'response')) must be set up BEFORE page.goto()\n- pollEngineState polls window._gameEngine; debug page tests use DOM selectors only\n- `parseSimMinutes(text)` helper in m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js for clock comparisons\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #159
--- ## Status DONE ## What Changed - **`tests/helpers.js`** — Added `waitForDashboard()` shared helper; exported it alongside existing helpers - **`tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js`** — Removed local `waitForDashboard` def, imported from helpers; added `parseSimMinutes()` utility; added 5 new tests: tile position display, facing direction, target field when walking, reset button behavior, 2x speed clock advancement - **`tests/regression/ai-decisions.spec.js`** — Added test verifying LLM responses include `dialogue` and `description` fields (spec gap: "decisions include dialogue text and description") - **`tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js`** — Removed local `waitForDashboard` def, imported from helpers; added test verifying kotatsu/tv can show 2 slots simultaneously occupied - **`tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js`** — Removed local `waitForDashboard` def, imported from helpers - **`tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js`** — Removed local `waitForDashboard` def, imported from helpers - **`tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js`** — Extended `interactTimerMax` poll from 20s→30s to fix pre-existing intermittent timeout - **`README.md`** — Updated test counts (81→88), feature coverage map, structure docs ## Key Decisions - **Reset test**: waited for ≥5 log entries before resetting (not just >0) to ensure the post-reset count is clearly smaller even if the new sim fires 1–2 events immediately - **Speed test**: compared sim-minute advancement over 5s at 1x vs 2x; asserted delta2x > delta1x × 1.4 to tolerate timing variance — observed clean 5 vs 10 sim-min in practice - **Multi-cap test**: graceful skip pattern (like REJECT/TALK tests) since it requires AI to direct two characters to the same object non-deterministically — test passed in this run (tv: 2/2 occupied) - **LLM dialogue test**: assertive (not graceful skip) since the spec says dialogue/description are always included in decisions; confirmed all 3 initial decisions included both fields ## PR / Branch Pushed directly to `main` (beee82d)
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "---\n\n## Status\nDONE\n\n## What Changed\n- **`tests/helpers.js`** \u2014 Added `waitForDashboard()` shared helper; exported it alongside existing helpers\n- **`tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js`** \u2014 Removed local `waitForDashboard` def, imported from helpers; added `parseSimMinutes()` utility; added 5 new tests: tile position display, facing direction, target field when walking, reset button behavior, 2x speed clock advancement\n- **`tests/regression/ai-decisions.spec.js`** \u2014 Added test verifying LLM responses include `dialogue` and `description` fields (spec gap: \"decisions include dialogue text and description\")\n- **`tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js`** \u2014 Removed local `waitForDashboard` def, imported from helpers; added test verifying kotatsu/tv can show 2 slots simultaneously occupied\n- **`tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js`** \u2014 Removed local `waitForDashboard` def, imported from helpers\n- **`tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js`** \u2014 Removed local `waitForDashboard` def, imported from helpers\n- **`tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js`** \u2014 Extended `interactTimerMax` poll from 20s\u219230s to fix pre-existing intermittent timeout\n- **`README.md`** \u2014 Updated test counts (81\u219288), feature coverage map, structure docs\n\n## Key Decisions\n- **Reset test**: waited for \u22655 log entries before resetting (not just >0) to ensure the post-reset count is clearly smaller even if the new sim fires 1\u20132 events immediately\n- **Speed test**: compared sim-minute advancement over 5s at 1x vs 2x; asserted delta2x > delta1x \u00d7 1.4 to tolerate timing variance \u2014 observed clean 5 vs 10 sim-min in practice\n- **Multi-cap test**: graceful skip pattern (like REJECT/TALK tests) since it requires AI to direct two characters to the same object non-deterministically \u2014 test passed in this run (tv: 2/2 occupied)\n- **LLM dialogue test**: assertive (not graceful skip) since the spec says dialogue/description are always included in decisions; confirmed all 3 initial decisions included both fields\n\n## PR / Branch\nPushed directly to `main` (beee82d)"
  }
}
